MAT_3_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_3_15.text: Let it be so now. It is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness in this way.
MAT_3_15.normalized: let it be so now it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness in this way

MAT_4_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_4_4.text: It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.
MAT_4_4.normalized: it is written man shall not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of god

MAT_4_7.speaker: Jesus
MAT_4_7.text: It is also written: 'Do not put the Lord your God to the test.
MAT_4_7.normalized: it is also written do not put the lord your god to the test

MAT_4_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_4_10.text: Away from Me, Satan! For it is written: 'Worship the Lord your God and serve Him only.
MAT_4_10.normalized: away from me satan for it is written worship the lord your god and serve him only

MAT_4_17.speaker: Jesus
MAT_4_17.text: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.
MAT_4_17.normalized: repent for the kingdom of heaven is near

MAT_4_19.speaker: Jesus
MAT_4_19.text: Come, follow Me, and I will make you fishers of men.
MAT_4_19.normalized: come follow me and i will make you fishers of men

MAT_5_3.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_3.text: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_3.normalized: blessed are the poor in spirit for theirs is the kingdom of heaven

MAT_5_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_4.text: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
MAT_5_4.normalized: blessed are those who mourn for they will be comforted

MAT_5_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_5.text: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.
MAT_5_5.normalized: blessed are the meek for they will inherit the earth

MAT_5_6.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_6.text: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.
MAT_5_6.normalized: blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness for they will be filled

MAT_5_7.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_7.text: Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.
MAT_5_7.normalized: blessed are the merciful for they will be shown mercy

MAT_5_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_8.text: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
MAT_5_8.normalized: blessed are the pure in heart for they will see god

MAT_5_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_9.text: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called sons of God.
MAT_5_9.normalized: blessed are the peacemakers for they will be called sons of god

MAT_5_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_10.text: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_10.normalized: blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness for theirs is the kingdom of heaven

MAT_5_11.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_11.text: Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you, and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of Me.
MAT_5_11.normalized: blessed are you when people insult you persecute you and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of me

MAT_5_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_12.text: Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets before you.
MAT_5_12.normalized: rejoice and be glad because great is your reward in heaven for in the same way they persecuted the prophets before you

MAT_5_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_13.text: You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its savor, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled by men.
MAT_5_13.normalized: you are the salt of the earth but if the salt loses its savor how can it be made salty again it is no longer good for anything except to be thrown out and trampled by men

MAT_5_14.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_14.text: You are the light of the world. A city on a hill cannot be hidden.
MAT_5_14.normalized: you are the light of the world a city on a hill cannot be hidden

MAT_5_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_15.text: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they set it on a stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.
MAT_5_15.normalized: neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket instead they set it on a stand and it gives light to everyone in the house

MAT_5_16.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_16.text: In the same way, let your light shine before men, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
MAT_5_16.normalized: in the same way let your light shine before men that they may see your good deeds and glorify your father in heaven

MAT_5_17.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_17.text: Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets. I have not come to abolish them, but to fulfill them.
MAT_5_17.normalized: do not think that i have come to abolish the law or the prophets i have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them

MAT_5_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_18.text: For I tell you truly, until heaven and earth pass away, not a single jot, not a stroke of a pen, will disappear from the Law until everything is accomplished.
MAT_5_18.normalized: for i tell you truly until heaven and earth pass away not a single jot not a stroke of a pen will disappear from the law until everything is accomplished

MAT_5_19.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_19.text: So then, whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do likewise will be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever practices and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_19.normalized: so then whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do likewise will be called least in the kingdom of heaven but whoever practices and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven

MAT_5_20.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_20.text: For I tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_20.normalized: for i tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and pharisees you will never enter the kingdom of heaven

MAT_5_21.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_21.text: You have heard that it was said to the ancients, 'Do not murder' and 'Anyone who murders will be subject to judgment.
MAT_5_21.normalized: you have heard that it was said to the ancients do not murder and anyone who murders will be subject to judgment

MAT_5_22.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_22.text: But I tell you that anyone who is angry with his brother will be subject to judgment. Again, anyone who says to his brother, 'Raca,' will be subject to the Sanhedrin. But anyone who says, 'You fool!' will be subject to the fire of hell.
MAT_5_22.normalized: but i tell you that anyone who is angry with his brother will be subject to judgment again anyone who says to his brother raca will be subject to the sanhedrin but anyone who says you fool will be subject to the fire of hell

MAT_5_23.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_23.text: So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
MAT_5_23.normalized: so if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you

MAT_5_24.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_24.text: leave your gift there before the altar. First go and be reconciled to your brother; then come and offer your gift.
MAT_5_24.normalized: leave your gift there before the altar first go and be reconciled to your brother then come and offer your gift

MAT_5_25.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_25.text: Reconcile quickly with your adversary, while you are still on the way to court. Otherwise, he may hand you over to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and you may be thrown into prison.
MAT_5_25.normalized: reconcile quickly with your adversary while you are still on the way to court otherwise he may hand you over to the judge and the judge may hand you over to the officer and you may be thrown into prison

MAT_5_26.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_26.text: Truly I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny.
MAT_5_26.normalized: truly i tell you you will not get out until you have paid the last penny

MAT_5_27.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_27.text: You have heard that it was said, 'Do not commit adultery.
MAT_5_27.normalized: you have heard that it was said do not commit adultery

MAT_5_28.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_28.text: But I tell you that anyone who looks at a woman to lust after her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
MAT_5_28.normalized: but i tell you that anyone who looks at a woman to lust after her has already committed adultery with her in his heart

MAT_5_29.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_29.text: If your right eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
MAT_5_29.normalized: if your right eye causes you to sin gouge it out and throw it away it is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell

MAT_5_30.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_30.text: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to depart into hell.
MAT_5_30.normalized: and if your right hand causes you to sin cut it off and throw it away it is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to depart into hell

MAT_5_31.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_31.text: It has also been said, 'Whoever divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce.
MAT_5_31.normalized: it has also been said whoever divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce

MAT_5_32.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_32.text: But I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, brings adultery upon her. And he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
MAT_5_32.normalized: but i tell you that anyone who divorces his wife except for sexual immorality brings adultery upon her and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery

MAT_5_33.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_33.text: Again, you have heard that it was said to the ancients, 'Do not break your oath, but fulfill your vows to the Lord.
MAT_5_33.normalized: again you have heard that it was said to the ancients do not break your oath but fulfill your vows to the lord

MAT_5_34.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_34.text: But I tell you not to swear at all: either by heaven, for it is God's throne;
MAT_5_34.normalized: but i tell you not to swear at all either by heaven for it is god s throne

MAT_5_35.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_35.text: or by the earth, for it is His footstool; or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
MAT_5_35.normalized: or by the earth for it is his footstool or by jerusalem for it is the city of the great king

MAT_5_36.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_36.text: Nor should you swear by your head, for you cannot make a single hair white or black.
MAT_5_36.normalized: nor should you swear by your head for you cannot make a single hair white or black

MAT_5_37.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_37.text: Simply let your 'Yes' be 'Yes,' and your 'No,' 'No.' Anything more comes from the evil one.
MAT_5_37.normalized: simply let your yes be yes and your no no anything more comes from the evil one

MAT_5_38.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_38.text: You have heard that it was said, 'Eye for eye and tooth for tooth.
MAT_5_38.normalized: you have heard that it was said eye for eye and tooth for tooth

MAT_5_39.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_39.text: But I tell you not to resist an evil person. If someone slaps you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also;
MAT_5_39.normalized: but i tell you not to resist an evil person if someone slaps you on your right cheek turn to him the other also

MAT_5_40.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_40.text: if someone wants to sue you and take your tunic, let him have your cloak as well;
MAT_5_40.normalized: if someone wants to sue you and take your tunic let him have your cloak as well

MAT_5_41.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_41.text: and if someone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
MAT_5_41.normalized: and if someone forces you to go one mile go with him two miles

MAT_5_42.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_42.text: Give to the one who asks you, and do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you.
MAT_5_42.normalized: give to the one who asks you and do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you

MAT_5_43.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_43.text: You have heard that it was said, 'Love your neighbor' and 'Hate your enemy.
MAT_5_43.normalized: you have heard that it was said love your neighbor and hate your enemy

MAT_5_44.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_44.text: But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
MAT_5_44.normalized: but i tell you love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you

MAT_5_45.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_45.text: that you may be sons of your Father in heaven. He causes His sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.
MAT_5_45.normalized: that you may be sons of your father in heaven he causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous

MAT_5_46.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_46.text: If you love those who love you, what reward will you get? Do not even tax collectors do the same?
MAT_5_46.normalized: if you love those who love you what reward will you get do not even tax collectors do the same

MAT_5_47.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_47.text: And if you greet only your brothers, what are you doing more than others? Do not even Gentiles do the same?
MAT_5_47.normalized: and if you greet only your brothers what are you doing more than others do not even gentiles do the same

MAT_5_48.speaker: Jesus
MAT_5_48.text: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
MAT_5_48.normalized: be perfect therefore as your heavenly father is perfect

MAT_6_1.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_1.text: Be careful not to perform your righteous acts before men to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.
MAT_6_1.normalized: be careful not to perform your righteous acts before men to be seen by them if you do you will have no reward from your father in heaven

MAT_6_2.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_2.text: So when you give to the needy, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by men. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.
MAT_6_2.normalized: so when you give to the needy do not sound a trumpet before you as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets to be honored by men truly i tell you they already have their full reward

MAT_6_3.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_3.text: But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
MAT_6_3.normalized: but when you give to the needy do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing

MAT_6_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_4.text: so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
MAT_6_4.normalized: so that your giving may be in secret and your father who sees what is done in secret will reward you

MAT_6_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_5.text: And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites. For they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by men. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.
MAT_6_5.normalized: and when you pray do not be like the hypocrites for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by men truly i tell you they already have their full reward

MAT_6_6.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_6.text: But when you pray, go into your inner room, shut your door, and pray to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
MAT_6_6.normalized: but when you pray go into your inner room shut your door and pray to your father who is unseen and your father who sees what is done in secret will reward you

MAT_6_7.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_7.text: And when you pray, do not babble on like pagans, for they think that by their many words they will be heard.
MAT_6_7.normalized: and when you pray do not babble on like pagans for they think that by their many words they will be heard

MAT_6_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_8.text: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask Him.
MAT_6_8.normalized: do not be like them for your father knows what you need before you ask him

MAT_6_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_9.text: So then, this is how you should pray: 'Our Father in heaven, hallowed be Your name.
MAT_6_9.normalized: so then this is how you should pray our father in heaven hallowed be your name

MAT_6_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_10.text: Your kingdom come, Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
MAT_6_10.normalized: your kingdom come your will be done on earth as it is in heaven

MAT_6_11.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_11.text: Give us this day our daily bread.
MAT_6_11.normalized: give us this day our daily bread

MAT_6_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_12.text: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
MAT_6_12.normalized: and forgive us our debts as we also have forgiven our debtors

MAT_6_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_13.text: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.
MAT_6_13.normalized: and lead us not into temptation but deliver us from the evil one

MAT_6_14.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_14.text: For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
MAT_6_14.normalized: for if you forgive men their trespasses your heavenly father will also forgive you

MAT_6_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_15.text: But if you do not forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive yours.
MAT_6_15.normalized: but if you do not forgive men their trespasses neither will your father forgive yours

MAT_6_16.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_16.text: When you fast, do not be somber like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces to show men they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.
MAT_6_16.normalized: when you fast do not be somber like the hypocrites for they disfigure their faces to show men they are fasting truly i tell you they already have their full reward

MAT_6_17.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_17.text: But when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,
MAT_6_17.normalized: but when you fast anoint your head and wash your face

MAT_6_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_18.text: so that your fasting will not be obvious to men, but only to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
MAT_6_18.normalized: so that your fasting will not be obvious to men but only to your father who is unseen and your father who sees what is done in secret will reward you

MAT_6_19.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_19.text: Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.
MAT_6_19.normalized: do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal

MAT_6_20.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_20.text: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moth and rust do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal.
MAT_6_20.normalized: but store up for yourselves treasures in heaven where moth and rust do not destroy and where thieves do not break in and steal

MAT_6_21.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_21.text: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
MAT_6_21.normalized: for where your treasure is there your heart will be also

MAT_6_22.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_22.text: The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be full of light.
MAT_6_22.normalized: the eye is the lamp of the body if your eyes are good your whole body will be full of light

MAT_6_23.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_23.text: But if your eyes are bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness!
MAT_6_23.normalized: but if your eyes are bad your whole body will be full of darkness if then the light within you is darkness how great is that darkness

MAT_6_24.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_24.text: No one can serve two masters: Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
MAT_6_24.normalized: no one can serve two masters either he will hate the one and love the other or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other you cannot serve both god and money

MAT_6_25.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_25.text: Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes?
MAT_6_25.normalized: therefore i tell you do not worry about your life what you will eat or drink or about your body what you will wear is not life more than food and the body more than clothes

MAT_6_26.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_26.text: Look at the birds of the air: They do not sow or reap or gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?
MAT_6_26.normalized: look at the birds of the air they do not sow or reap or gather into barns and yet your heavenly father feeds them are you not much more valuable than they

MAT_6_27.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_27.text: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?
MAT_6_27.normalized: who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life

MAT_6_28.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_28.text: And why do you worry about clothes? Consider how the lilies of the field grow: They do not labor or spin.
MAT_6_28.normalized: and why do you worry about clothes consider how the lilies of the field grow they do not labor or spin

MAT_6_29.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_29.text: Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these.
MAT_6_29.normalized: yet i tell you that not even solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these

MAT_6_30.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_30.text: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?
MAT_6_30.normalized: if that is how god clothes the grass of the field which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace will he not much more clothe you o you of little faith

MAT_6_31.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_31.text: Therefore do not worry, saying, 'What shall we eat?' or 'What shall we drink?' or 'What shall we wear?
MAT_6_31.normalized: therefore do not worry saying what shall we eat or what shall we drink or what shall we wear

MAT_6_32.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_32.text: For the Gentiles strive after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
MAT_6_32.normalized: for the gentiles strive after all these things and your heavenly father knows that you need them

MAT_6_33.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_33.text: But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things will be added unto you.
MAT_6_33.normalized: but seek first the kingdom of god and his righteousness and all these things will be added unto you

MAT_6_34.speaker: Jesus
MAT_6_34.text: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Today has enough trouble of its own.
MAT_6_34.normalized: therefore do not worry about tomorrow for tomorrow will worry about itself today has enough trouble of its own

MAT_7_1.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_1.text: Do not judge, or you will be judged.
MAT_7_1.normalized: do not judge or you will be judged

MAT_7_2.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_2.text: For with the same judgment you pronounce, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
MAT_7_2.normalized: for with the same judgment you pronounce you will be judged and with the measure you use it will be measured to you

MAT_7_3.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_3.text: Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?
MAT_7_3.normalized: why do you look at the speck in your brother s eye but fail to notice the beam in your own eye

MAT_7_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_4.text: How can you say to your brother, 'Let me take the speck out of your eye,' while there is still a beam in your own eye?
MAT_7_4.normalized: how can you say to your brother let me take the speck out of your eye while there is still a beam in your own eye

MAT_7_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_5.text: You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
MAT_7_5.normalized: you hypocrite first take the beam out of your own eye and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother s eye

MAT_7_6.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_6.text: Do not give dogs what is holy; do not throw your pearls before swine. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and then turn and tear you to pieces.
MAT_7_6.normalized: do not give dogs what is holy do not throw your pearls before swine if you do they may trample them under their feet and then turn and tear you to pieces

MAT_7_7.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_7.text: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.
MAT_7_7.normalized: ask and it will be given to you seek and you will find knock and the door will be opened to you

MAT_7_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_8.text: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.
MAT_7_8.normalized: for everyone who asks receives he who seeks finds and to him who knocks the door will be opened

MAT_7_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_9.text: Which of you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
MAT_7_9.normalized: which of you if his son asks for bread will give him a stone

MAT_7_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_10.text: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
MAT_7_10.normalized: or if he asks for a fish will give him a snake

MAT_7_11.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_11.text: So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!
MAT_7_11.normalized: so if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children how much more will your father in heaven give good things to those who ask him

MAT_7_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_12.text: In everything, then, do to others as you would have them do to you. For this is the essence of the Law and the Prophets.
MAT_7_12.normalized: in everything then do to others as you would have them do to you for this is the essence of the law and the prophets

MAT_7_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_13.text: Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.
MAT_7_13.normalized: enter through the narrow gate for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction and many enter through it

MAT_7_14.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_14.text: But small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life, and only a few find it.
MAT_7_14.normalized: but small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life and only a few find it

MAT_7_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_7_15.text: Beware of false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.
MAT_7_15.normalized: beware of false prophets they come to you in sheep s clothing but inwardly they are ravenous wolves

MAT_7_16.speaker: Jesus

MAT_24_18.normalized: and let no one in the field return for his cloak

MAT_24_19.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_19.text: How miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers!
MAT_24_19.normalized: how miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers

MAT_24_20.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_20.text: Pray that your flight will not occur in the winter or on the Sabbath.
MAT_24_20.normalized: pray that your flight will not occur in the winter or on the sabbath

MAT_24_21.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_21.text: For at that time there will be great tribulation, unseen from the beginning of the world until now, and never to be seen again.
MAT_24_21.normalized: for at that time there will be great tribulation unseen from the beginning of the world until now and never to be seen again

MAT_24_22.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_22.text: If those days had not been cut short, nobody would be saved. But for the sake of the elect, those days will be cut short.
MAT_24_22.normalized: if those days had not been cut short nobody would be saved but for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short

MAT_24_23.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_23.text: At that time, if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or 'There He is!' do not believe it.
MAT_24_23.normalized: at that time if anyone says to you look here is the christ or there he is do not believe it

MAT_24_24.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_24.text: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform great signs and wonders to deceive even the elect, if that were possible.
MAT_24_24.normalized: for false christs and false prophets will appear and perform great signs and wonders to deceive even the elect if that were possible

MAT_24_25.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_25.text: See, I have told you in advance.
MAT_24_25.normalized: see i have told you in advance

MAT_24_26.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_26.text: So if they tell you, 'There He is, in the wilderness,' do not go out, or, 'Here He is, in the inner rooms,' do not believe it.
MAT_24_26.normalized: so if they tell you there he is in the wilderness do not go out or here he is in the inner rooms do not believe it

MAT_24_27.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_27.text: For just as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
MAT_24_27.normalized: for just as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west so will be the coming of the son of man

MAT_24_28.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_28.text: Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.
MAT_24_28.normalized: wherever there is a carcass there the vultures will gather

MAT_24_29.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_29.text: Immediately after the tribulation of those days: 'The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
MAT_24_29.normalized: immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened and the moon will not give its light the stars will fall from the sky and the powers of the heavens will be shaken

MAT_24_30.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_30.text: At that time the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn. They will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
MAT_24_30.normalized: at that time the sign of the son of man will appear in heaven and all the tribes of the earth will mourn they will see the son of man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory

MAT_24_31.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_31.text: And He will send out His angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather His elect from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other.
MAT_24_31.normalized: and he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call and they will gather his elect from the four winds from one end of the heavens to the other

MAT_24_32.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_32.text: Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its branches become tender and sprout leaves, you know that summer is near.
MAT_24_32.normalized: now learn this lesson from the fig tree as soon as its branches become tender and sprout leaves you know that summer is near

MAT_24_33.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_33.text: So also, when you see all these things, you will know that He is near, right at the door.
MAT_24_33.normalized: so also when you see all these things you will know that he is near right at the door

MAT_24_34.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_34.text: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.
MAT_24_34.normalized: truly i tell you this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened

MAT_24_35.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_35.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.
MAT_24_35.normalized: heaven and earth will pass away but my words will never pass away

MAT_24_36.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_36.text: No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
MAT_24_36.normalized: no one knows about that day or hour not even the angels in heaven nor the son but only the father

MAT_24_37.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_37.text: As it was in the days of Noah, so will it be at the coming of the Son of Man.
MAT_24_37.normalized: as it was in the days of noah so will it be at the coming of the son of man

MAT_24_38.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_38.text: For in the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark.
MAT_24_38.normalized: for in the days before the flood people were eating and drinking marrying and giving in marriage up to the day noah entered the ark

MAT_24_39.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_39.text: And they were oblivious until the flood came and swept them all away. So will it be at the coming of the Son of Man.
MAT_24_39.normalized: and they were oblivious until the flood came and swept them all away so will it be at the coming of the son of man

MAT_24_40.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_40.text: Two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left.
MAT_24_40.normalized: two men will be in the field one will be taken and the other left

MAT_24_41.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_41.text: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.
MAT_24_41.normalized: two women will be grinding at the mill one will be taken and the other left

MAT_24_42.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_42.text: Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day on which your Lord will come.
MAT_24_42.normalized: therefore keep watch because you do not know the day on which your lord will come

MAT_24_43.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_43.text: But understand this: If the homeowner had known in which watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have kept watch and would not have let his house be broken into.
MAT_24_43.normalized: but understand this if the homeowner had known in which watch of the night the thief was coming he would have kept watch and would not have let his house be broken into

MAT_24_44.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_44.text: For this reason, you also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you do not expect.
MAT_24_44.normalized: for this reason you also must be ready because the son of man will come at an hour you do not expect

MAT_24_45.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_45.text: Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom the master has put in charge of his household, to give the others their food at the proper time?
MAT_24_45.normalized: who then is the faithful and wise servant whom the master has put in charge of his household to give the others their food at the proper time

MAT_24_46.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_46.text: Blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns.
MAT_24_46.normalized: blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns

MAT_24_47.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_47.text: Truly I tell you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
MAT_24_47.normalized: truly i tell you he will put him in charge of all his possessions

MAT_24_48.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_48.text: But suppose that servant is wicked and says in his heart, 'My master will be away a long time.
MAT_24_48.normalized: but suppose that servant is wicked and says in his heart my master will be away a long time

MAT_24_49.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_49.text: And he begins to beat his fellow servants and to eat and drink with drunkards.
MAT_24_49.normalized: and he begins to beat his fellow servants and to eat and drink with drunkards

MAT_24_50.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_50.text: The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate.
MAT_24_50.normalized: the master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate

MAT_24_51.speaker: Jesus
MAT_24_51.text: Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
MAT_24_51.normalized: then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth

MAT_25_1.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_1.text: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
MAT_25_1.normalized: at that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom

MAT_25_2.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_2.text: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
MAT_25_2.normalized: five of them were foolish and five were wise

MAT_25_3.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_3.text: The foolish ones took their lamps but did not take along any extra oil.
MAT_25_3.normalized: the foolish ones took their lamps but did not take along any extra oil

MAT_25_4.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_4.text: But the wise ones took oil in flasks along with their lamps.
MAT_25_4.normalized: but the wise ones took oil in flasks along with their lamps

MAT_25_5.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_5.text: When the bridegroom was delayed, they all became drowsy and fell asleep.
MAT_25_5.normalized: when the bridegroom was delayed they all became drowsy and fell asleep

MAT_25_6.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_6.text: At midnight the cry rang out: 'Here is the bridegroom! Come out to meet him!
MAT_25_6.normalized: at midnight the cry rang out here is the bridegroom come out to meet him

MAT_25_7.text: Then all the virgins woke up and trimmed their lamps.
MAT_25_7.normalized: then all the virgins woke up and trimmed their lamps

MAT_25_8.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_8.text: The foolish ones said to the wise, 'Give us some of your oil; our lamps are going out.
MAT_25_8.normalized: the foolish ones said to the wise give us some of your oil our lamps are going out

MAT_25_9.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_9.text: No,' said the wise ones, 'or there may not be enough for both us and you. Instead, go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
MAT_25_9.normalized: no said the wise ones or there may not be enough for both us and you instead go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves

MAT_25_10.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_10.text: But while they were on their way to buy it, the bridegroom arrived. Those who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet, and the door was shut.
MAT_25_10.normalized: but while they were on their way to buy it the bridegroom arrived those who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet and the door was shut

MAT_25_11.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_11.text: Later the other virgins arrived and said, 'Lord, lord, open the door for us!
MAT_25_11.normalized: later the other virgins arrived and said lord lord open the door for us

MAT_25_12.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_12.text: But he replied, 'Truly I tell you, I do not know you.

MAT_25_13.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_13.text: Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day or the hour.

MAT_25_14.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_14.text: For it is just like a man going on a journey, who called his servants and entrusted them with his possessions.
MAT_25_14.normalized: for it is just like a man going on a journey who called his servants and entrusted them with his possessions

MAT_25_15.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_15.text: To one he gave five talents, to another two talents, and to another one talent-each according to his own ability. And he went on his journey.
MAT_25_15.normalized: to one he gave five talents to another two talents and to another one talent each according to his own ability and he went on his journey

MAT_25_16.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_16.text: The servant who had received the five talents went at once and put them to work and gained five more.
MAT_25_16.normalized: the servant who had received the five talents went at once and put them to work and gained five more

MAT_25_17.text: Likewise, the one with the two talents gained two more.

MAT_25_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_18.text: But the servant who had received the one talent went off, dug a hole in the ground, and hid his master's money.
MAT_25_18.normalized: but the servant who had received the one talent went off dug a hole in the ground and hid his master s money

MAT_25_19.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_19.text: After a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
MAT_25_19.normalized: after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them

MAT_25_20.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_20.text: The servant who had received the five talents came and presented five more. 'Master,' he said, 'you entrusted me with five talents. See, I have gained five more.
MAT_25_20.normalized: the servant who had received the five talents came and presented five more master he said you entrusted me with five talents see i have gained five more

MAT_25_21.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_21.text: His master replied, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Enter into the joy of your master!
MAT_25_21.normalized: his master replied well done good and faithful servant you have been faithful with a few things i will put you in charge of many things enter into the joy of your master

MAT_25_22.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_22.text: The servant who had received the two talents also came and said, 'Master, you entrusted me with two talents. See, I have gained two more.
MAT_25_22.normalized: the servant who had received the two talents also came and said master you entrusted me with two talents see i have gained two more

MAT_25_23.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_23.text: His master replied, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Enter into the joy of your master!
MAT_25_23.normalized: his master replied well done good and faithful servant you have been faithful with a few things i will put you in charge of many things enter into the joy of your master

MAT_25_24.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_24.text: Finally, the servant who had received the one talent came and said, 'Master, I knew that you are a hard man, reaping where you have not sown and gathering where you have not scattered seed.
MAT_25_24.normalized: finally the servant who had received the one talent came and said master i knew that you are a hard man reaping where you have not sown and gathering where you have not scattered seed

MAT_25_25.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_25.text: So I was afraid and went out and hid your talent in the ground. See, you have what belongs to you.
MAT_25_25.normalized: so i was afraid and went out and hid your talent in the ground see you have what belongs to you

MAT_25_26.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_26.text: You wicked, lazy servant!' replied his master. 'You knew that I reap where I have not sown and gather where I have not scattered seed.
MAT_25_26.normalized: you wicked lazy servant replied his master you knew that i reap where i have not sown and gather where i have not scattered seed

MAT_25_27.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_27.text: Then you should have deposited my money with the bankers, and on my return I would have received it back with interest.
MAT_25_27.normalized: then you should have deposited my money with the bankers and on my return i would have received it back with interest

MAT_25_28.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_28.text: Therefore take the talent from him and give it to the one who has ten talents.

MAT_25_29.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_29.text: For everyone who has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. But the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

MAT_25_30.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_30.text: And throw that worthless servant into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
MAT_25_30.normalized: and throw that worthless servant into the outer darkness where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth

MAT_25_31.text: When the Son of Man comes in His glory, and all the angels with Him, He will sit on His glorious throne.
MAT_25_31.normalized: when the son of man comes in his glory and all the angels with him he will sit on his glorious throne

MAT_25_32.text: All the nations will be gathered before Him, and He will separate the people one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.

MAT_25_33.text: He will place the sheep on His right and the goats on His left.

MAT_25_34.text: Then the King will say to those on His right, 'Come, you who are blessed by My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
MAT_25_34.normalized: then the king will say to those on his right come you who are blessed by my father inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world

MAT_25_35.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_35.text: For I was hungry and you gave Me something to eat, I was thirsty and you gave Me something to drink, I was a stranger and you took Me in,
MAT_25_35.normalized: for i was hungry and you gave me something to eat i was thirsty and you gave me something to drink i was a stranger and you took me in

MAT_25_36.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_36.text: I was naked and you clothed Me, I was sick and you looked after Me, I was in prison and you visited Me.

MAT_25_37.text: Then the righteous will answer Him, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry and feed You, or thirsty and give You something to drink?
MAT_25_37.normalized: then the righteous will answer him lord when did we see you hungry and feed you or thirsty and give you something to drink

MAT_25_38.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_38.text: When did we see You a stranger and take You in, or naked and clothe You?

MAT_25_39.text: When did we see You sick or in prison and visit You?
MAT_25_39.normalized: when did we see you sick or in prison and visit you

MAT_25_40.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_40.text: And the King will reply, 'Truly I tell you, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers of Mine, you did for Me.

MAT_25_41.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_41.text: Then He will say to those on His left, 'Depart from Me, you who are cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.

MAT_25_42.text: For I was hungry and you gave Me nothing to eat, I was thirsty and you gave Me nothing to drink,
MAT_25_42.normalized: for i was hungry and you gave me nothing to eat i was thirsty and you gave me nothing to drink

MAT_25_43.text: I was a stranger and you did not take Me in, I was naked and you did not clothe Me, I was sick and in prison and you did not look after Me.

MAT_25_44.speaker: Jesus
MAT_25_44.text: And they too will reply, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to You?
MAT_25_44.normalized: and they too will reply lord when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and did not minister to you

MAT_25_45.text: Then the King will answer, 'Truly I tell you, whatever you did not do for one of the least of these, you did not do for Me.
MAT_25_45.normalized: then the king will answer truly i tell you whatever you did not do for one of the least of these you did not do for me

MAT_25_46.text: And they will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.

MAT_26_18.speaker: Jesus
MAT_26_18.text: Go into the city to a certain man and tell him that the Teacher says, 'My time is near. I will keep the Passover with My disciples at your house.

MAT_26_29.text: I tell you, I will not drink of this fruit of the vine from now on until that day when I drink it anew with you in My Father's kingdom.

MRK_12_39.normalized: and to have the chief seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets

MRK_12_40.speaker: Jesus
MRK_12_40.text: They defraud widows of their houses, and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will receive greater condemnation.
MRK_12_40.normalized: they defraud widows of their houses and for a show make lengthy prayers these men will receive greater condemnation

MRK_12_43.speaker: Jesus
MRK_12_43.text: Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put more than all the others into the treasury.
MRK_12_43.normalized: truly i tell you this poor widow has put more than all the others into the treasury

MRK_12_44.speaker: Jesus
MRK_12_44.text: For they all contributed out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.
MRK_12_44.normalized: for they all contributed out of their surplus but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on

MRK_13_2.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_2.text: Do you see all these great buildings? Not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.
MRK_13_2.normalized: do you see all these great buildings not one stone here will be left on another every one will be thrown down

MRK_13_5.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_5.text: See to it that no one deceives you.
MRK_13_5.normalized: see to it that no one deceives you

MRK_13_6.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_6.text: Many will come in My name, claiming, 'I am He,' and will deceive many.
MRK_13_6.normalized: many will come in my name claiming i am he and will deceive many

MRK_13_7.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_7.text: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. These things must happen, but the end is still to come.
MRK_13_7.normalized: when you hear of wars and rumors of wars do not be alarmed these things must happen but the end is still to come

MRK_13_8.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_8.text: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, as well as famines. These are the beginning of birth pains.
MRK_13_8.normalized: nation will rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom there will be earthquakes in various places as well as famines these are the beginning of birth pains

MRK_13_9.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_9.text: So be on your guard. You will be delivered over to the councils and beaten in the synagogues. On My account you will stand before governors and kings as witnesses to them.
MRK_13_9.normalized: so be on your guard you will be delivered over to the councils and beaten in the synagogues on my account you will stand before governors and kings as witnesses to them

MRK_13_10.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_10.text: And the gospel must first be proclaimed to all the nations.
MRK_13_10.normalized: and the gospel must first be proclaimed to all the nations

MRK_13_11.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_11.text: But when they arrest you and hand you over, do not worry beforehand what to say. Instead, speak whatever you are given at that time, for it will not be you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
MRK_13_11.normalized: but when they arrest you and hand you over do not worry beforehand what to say instead speak whatever you are given at that time for it will not be you speaking but the holy spirit

MRK_13_12.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_12.text: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise against their parents and have them put to death.
MRK_13_12.normalized: brother will betray brother to death and a father his child children will rise against their parents and have them put to death

MRK_13_13.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_13.text: You will be hated by everyone because of My name, but the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.
MRK_13_13.normalized: you will be hated by everyone because of my name but the one who perseveres to the end will be saved

MRK_13_14.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_14.text: So when you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
MRK_13_14.normalized: so when you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be let the reader understand then let those who are in judea flee to the mountains

MRK_13_15.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_15.text: Let no one on the housetop go back inside to retrieve anything from his house.
MRK_13_15.normalized: let no one on the housetop go back inside to retrieve anything from his house

MRK_13_16.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_16.text: And let no one in the field return for his cloak.
MRK_13_16.normalized: and let no one in the field return for his cloak

MRK_13_17.text: How miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers!
MRK_13_17.normalized: how miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers

MRK_13_18.speaker: Jesus

MRK_13_18.normalized: pray that this will not occur in the winter

MRK_13_19.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_19.text: For those will be days of tribulation unseen from the beginning of God's creation until now, and never to be seen again.
MRK_13_19.normalized: for those will be days of tribulation unseen from the beginning of god s creation until now and never to be seen again

MRK_13_20.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_20.text: If the Lord had not cut short those days, nobody would be saved. But for the sake of the elect, whom He has chosen, He has cut them short.
MRK_13_20.normalized: if the lord had not cut short those days nobody would be saved but for the sake of the elect whom he has chosen he has cut them short

MRK_13_21.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_21.text: At that time if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or 'There He is!' do not believe it.
MRK_13_21.normalized: at that time if anyone says to you look here is the christ or there he is do not believe it

MRK_13_22.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_22.text: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and wonders to deceive even the elect, if that were possible.
MRK_13_22.normalized: for false christs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and wonders to deceive even the elect if that were possible

MRK_13_23.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_23.text: So be on your guard; I have told you everything in advance.
MRK_13_23.normalized: so be on your guard i have told you everything in advance

MRK_13_24.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_24.text: But in those days, after that tribulation: 'The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light;
MRK_13_24.normalized: but in those days after that tribulation the sun will be darkened and the moon will not give its light

MRK_13_25.text: the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

MRK_13_26.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_26.text: At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
MRK_13_26.normalized: at that time they will see the son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory

MRK_13_27.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_27.text: And He will send out the angels to gather His elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.

MRK_13_28.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_28.text: Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its branches become tender and sprout leaves, you know that summer is near.
MRK_13_28.normalized: now learn this lesson from the fig tree as soon as its branches become tender and sprout leaves you know that summer is near

MRK_13_29.text: So also, when you see these things happening, know that He is near, right at the door.
MRK_13_29.normalized: so also when you see these things happening know that he is near right at the door

MRK_13_30.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_30.text: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.

MRK_13_31.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_31.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.
MRK_13_31.normalized: heaven and earth will pass away but my words will never pass away

MRK_13_32.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_32.text: No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
MRK_13_32.normalized: no one knows about that day or hour not even the angels in heaven nor the son but only the father

MRK_13_33.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_33.text: Be on your guard and stay alert! For you do not know when the appointed time will come.
MRK_13_33.normalized: be on your guard and stay alert for you do not know when the appointed time will come

MRK_13_34.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_34.text: It is like a man going on a journey who left his house, put each servant in charge of his own task, and instructed the doorkeeper to keep watch.
MRK_13_34.normalized: it is like a man going on a journey who left his house put each servant in charge of his own task and instructed the doorkeeper to keep watch

MRK_13_35.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_35.text: Therefore keep watch, because you do not know when the master of the house will return-whether in the evening, at midnight, when the rooster crows, or in the morning.
MRK_13_35.normalized: therefore keep watch because you do not know when the master of the house will return whether in the evening at midnight when the rooster crows or in the morning

MRK_13_36.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_36.text: Otherwise, he may arrive without notice and find you sleeping.
MRK_13_36.normalized: otherwise he may arrive without notice and find you sleeping

MRK_13_37.speaker: Jesus
MRK_13_37.text: And what I say to you, I say to everyone: Keep watch!

LUK_6_21.normalized: blessed are you who hunger now for you will be filled blessed are you who weep now for you will laugh

LUK_6_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_22.text: Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude you and insult you and reject your name as evil because of the Son of Man.
LUK_6_22.normalized: blessed are you when people hate you and when they exclude you and insult you and reject your name as evil because of the son of man

LUK_6_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_23.text: Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, because great is your reward in heaven. For their fathers treated the prophets in the same way.
LUK_6_23.normalized: rejoice in that day and leap for joy because great is your reward in heaven for their fathers treated the prophets in the same way

LUK_6_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_24.text: But woe to you who are rich, for you have already received your comfort.
LUK_6_24.normalized: but woe to you who are rich for you have already received your comfort

LUK_6_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_25.text: Woe to you who are well fed now, for you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.
LUK_6_25.normalized: woe to you who are well fed now for you will hunger woe to you who laugh now for you will mourn and weep

LUK_6_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_26.text: Woe to you when all men speak well of you, for their fathers treated the false prophets in the same way.
LUK_6_26.normalized: woe to you when all men speak well of you for their fathers treated the false prophets in the same way

LUK_6_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_27.text: But to those of you who will listen, I say: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
LUK_6_27.normalized: but to those of you who will listen i say love your enemies do good to those who hate you

LUK_6_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_28.text: bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.
LUK_6_28.normalized: bless those who curse you pray for those who mistreat you

LUK_6_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_29.text: If someone strikes you on one cheek, turn to him the other also. And if someone takes your cloak, do not withhold your tunic as well.
LUK_6_29.normalized: if someone strikes you on one cheek turn to him the other also and if someone takes your cloak do not withhold your tunic as well

LUK_6_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_30.text: Give to everyone who asks you, and if anyone takes what is yours, do not demand it back.
LUK_6_30.normalized: give to everyone who asks you and if anyone takes what is yours do not demand it back

LUK_6_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_31.text: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
LUK_6_31.normalized: do to others as you would have them do to you

LUK_6_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_32.text: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners love those who love them.
LUK_6_32.normalized: if you love those who love you what credit is that to you even sinners love those who love them

LUK_6_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_33.text: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners do the same.
LUK_6_33.normalized: if you do good to those who do good to you what credit is that to you even sinners do the same

LUK_6_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_34.text: And if you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, expecting to be repaid in full.
LUK_6_34.normalized: and if you lend to those from whom you expect repayment what credit is that to you even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be repaid in full

LUK_6_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_35.text: But love your enemies, do good to them, and lend to them, expecting nothing in return. Then your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High; for He is kind to the ungrateful and wicked.
LUK_6_35.normalized: but love your enemies do good to them and lend to them expecting nothing in return then your reward will be great and you will be sons of the most high for he is kind to the ungrateful and wicked

LUK_6_36.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_36.text: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.
LUK_6_36.normalized: be merciful just as your father is merciful

LUK_6_37.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_37.text: Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
LUK_6_37.normalized: do not judge and you will not be judged do not condemn and you will not be condemned forgive and you will be forgiven

LUK_6_38.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_38.text: Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured back to you.
LUK_6_38.normalized: give and it will be given to you a good measure pressed down shaken together and running over will be poured into your lap for with the measure you use it will be measured back to you

LUK_6_39.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_39.text: Can a blind man lead a blind man? Will they not both fall into a pit?
LUK_6_39.normalized: can a blind man lead a blind man will they not both fall into a pit

LUK_6_40.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_40.text: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like his teacher.
LUK_6_40.normalized: a disciple is not above his teacher but everyone who is fully trained will be like his teacher

LUK_6_41.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_41.text: Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?
LUK_6_41.normalized: why do you look at the speck in your brother s eye but fail to notice the beam in your own eye

LUK_6_42.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_42.text: How can you say, 'Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye,' while you yourself fail to see the beam in your own eye? You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
LUK_6_42.normalized: how can you say brother let me take the speck out of your eye while you yourself fail to see the beam in your own eye you hypocrite first take the beam out of your own eye and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother s eye

LUK_6_43.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_43.text: No good tree bears bad fruit, nor does a bad tree bear good fruit.
LUK_6_43.normalized: no good tree bears bad fruit nor does a bad tree bear good fruit

LUK_6_44.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_44.text: For each tree is known by its own fruit. Indeed, figs are not gathered from thornbushes, nor grapes from brambles.
LUK_6_44.normalized: for each tree is known by its own fruit indeed figs are not gathered from thornbushes nor grapes from brambles

LUK_6_45.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_45.text: The good man brings good things out of the good treasure of his heart, and the evil man brings evil things out of the evil treasure of his heart. For out of the overflow of the heart the mouth speaks.
LUK_6_45.normalized: the good man brings good things out of the good treasure of his heart and the evil man brings evil things out of the evil treasure of his heart for out of the overflow of the heart the mouth speaks

LUK_6_46.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_46.text: Why do you call Me 'Lord, Lord,' but do not do what I say?
LUK_6_46.normalized: why do you call me lord lord but do not do what i say

LUK_6_47.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_47.text: I will show you what he is like who comes to Me and hears My words and acts on them:
LUK_6_47.normalized: i will show you what he is like who comes to me and hears my words and acts on them

LUK_6_48.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_48.text: He is like a man building a house, who dug down deep and laid his foundation on the rock. When the flood came, the torrent crashed against that house but could not shake it, because it was well built.
LUK_6_48.normalized: he is like a man building a house who dug down deep and laid his foundation on the rock when the flood came the torrent crashed against that house but could not shake it because it was well built

LUK_6_49.speaker: Jesus
LUK_6_49.text: But the one who hears My words and does not act on them is like a man who built his house on ground without a foundation. The torrent crashed against that house, and immediately it fell-and great was its destruction!
LUK_6_49.normalized: but the one who hears my words and does not act on them is like a man who built his house on ground without a foundation the torrent crashed against that house and immediately it fell and great was its destruction

LUK_7_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_9.text: I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such great faith.
LUK_7_9.normalized: i tell you not even in israel have i found such great faith

LUK_7_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_14.text: Young man, I tell you, get up!
LUK_7_14.normalized: young man i tell you get up

LUK_7_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_22.text: Go back and report to John what you have seen and heard: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.
LUK_7_22.normalized: go back and report to john what you have seen and heard the blind receive sight the lame walk the lepers are cleansed the deaf hear the dead are raised and the good news is preached to the poor

LUK_7_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_23.text: Blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of Me.
LUK_7_23.normalized: blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of me

LUK_7_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_24.text: What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?
LUK_7_24.normalized: what did you go out into the wilderness to see a reed swaying in the wind

LUK_7_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_25.text: Otherwise, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear elegant clothing and live in luxury are found in palaces.
LUK_7_25.normalized: otherwise what did you go out to see a man dressed in fine clothes look those who wear elegant clothing and live in luxury are found in palaces

LUK_7_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_26.text: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
LUK_7_26.normalized: what then did you go out to see a prophet yes i tell you and more than a prophet

LUK_7_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_27.text: This is the one about whom it is written: 'Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.
LUK_7_27.normalized: this is the one about whom it is written behold i will send my messenger ahead of you who will prepare your way before you

LUK_7_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_28.text: I tell you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John, yet even the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.
LUK_7_28.normalized: i tell you among those born of women there is no one greater than john yet even the least in the kingdom of god is greater than he

LUK_7_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_31.text: To what, then, can I compare the men of this generation? What are they like?
LUK_7_31.normalized: to what then can i compare the men of this generation what are they like

LUK_7_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_32.text: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling out to one another: 'We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not weep.
LUK_7_32.normalized: they are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling out to one another we played the flute for you and you did not dance we sang a dirge and you did not weep

LUK_7_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_33.text: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, 'He has a demon!
LUK_7_33.normalized: for john the baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine and you say he has a demon

LUK_7_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_34.text: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, 'Look at this glutton and drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!
LUK_7_34.normalized: the son of man came eating and drinking and you say look at this glutton and drunkard a friend of tax collectors and sinners

LUK_7_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_35.text: But wisdom is vindicated by all her children.
LUK_7_35.normalized: but wisdom is vindicated by all her children

LUK_7_40.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_40.text: Simon, I have something to tell you.
LUK_7_40.normalized: simon i have something to tell you

LUK_7_41.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_41.text: Two men were debtors to a certain moneylender. One owed him five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
LUK_7_41.normalized: two men were debtors to a certain moneylender one owed him five hundred denarii and the other fifty

LUK_7_42.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_42.text: When they were unable to repay him, he forgave both of them. Which one, then, will love him more?
LUK_7_42.normalized: when they were unable to repay him he forgave both of them which one then will love him more

LUK_7_44.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_44.text: Do you see this woman? When I entered your house, you did not give Me water for My feet, but she wet My feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
LUK_7_44.normalized: do you see this woman when i entered your house you did not give me water for my feet but she wet my feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair

LUK_7_45.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_45.text: You did not greet Me with a kiss, but she has not stopped kissing My feet since I arrived.
LUK_7_45.normalized: you did not greet me with a kiss but she has not stopped kissing my feet since i arrived

LUK_7_46.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_46.text: You did not anoint My head with oil, but she has anointed My feet with perfume.
LUK_7_46.normalized: you did not anoint my head with oil but she has anointed my feet with perfume

LUK_7_47.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_47.text: Therefore I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven, for she has loved much. But he who has been forgiven little loves little.
LUK_7_47.normalized: therefore i tell you her many sins have been forgiven for she has loved much but he who has been forgiven little loves little

LUK_7_50.speaker: Jesus
LUK_7_50.text: Your faith has saved you; go in peace.
LUK_7_50.normalized: your faith has saved you go in peace

LUK_8_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_5.text: A farmer went out to sow his seed. And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, where it was trampled, and the birds of the air devoured it.
LUK_8_5.normalized: a farmer went out to sow his seed and as he was sowing some seed fell along the path where it was trampled and the birds of the air devoured it

LUK_8_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_6.text: Some fell on rocky ground, and when it came up, the seedlings withered because they had no moisture.
LUK_8_6.normalized: some fell on rocky ground and when it came up the seedlings withered because they had no moisture

LUK_8_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_7.text: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up with it and choked the seedlings.
LUK_8_7.normalized: other seed fell among thorns which grew up with it and choked the seedlings

LUK_8_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_8.text: Still other seed fell on good soil, where it sprang up and produced a crop-a hundredfold. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
LUK_8_8.normalized: still other seed fell on good soil where it sprang up and produced a crop a hundredfold he who has ears to hear let him hear

LUK_8_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_10.text: The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to others I speak in parables, so that,'though seeing, they may not see; though hearing, they may not understand.
LUK_8_10.normalized: the knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of god has been given to you but to others i speak in parables so that though seeing they may not see though hearing they may not understand

LUK_8_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_11.text: Now this is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God.
LUK_8_11.normalized: now this is the meaning of the parable the seed is the word of god

LUK_8_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_12.text: The seeds along the path are those who hear, but the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
LUK_8_12.normalized: the seeds along the path are those who hear but the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts so that they may not believe and be saved

LUK_8_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_13.text: The seeds on rocky ground are those who hear the word and receive it with joy, but they have no root. They believe for a season, but in the time of testing, they fall away.
LUK_8_13.normalized: the seeds on rocky ground are those who hear the word and receive it with joy but they have no root they believe for a season but in the time of testing they fall away

LUK_8_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_14.text: The seeds that fell among the thorns are those who hear, but as they go on their way, they are choked by the worries, riches, and pleasures of this life, and their fruit does not mature.
LUK_8_14.normalized: the seeds that fell among the thorns are those who hear but as they go on their way they are choked by the worries riches and pleasures of this life and their fruit does not mature

LUK_8_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_15.text: But the seeds on good soil are those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, cling to it, and by persevering produce a crop.
LUK_8_15.normalized: but the seeds on good soil are those with a noble and good heart who hear the word cling to it and by persevering produce a crop

LUK_8_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_16.text: No one lights a lamp and covers it with a jar or puts it under a bed. Instead, he sets it on a stand, so those who enter can see the light.
LUK_8_16.normalized: no one lights a lamp and covers it with a jar or puts it under a bed instead he sets it on a stand so those who enter can see the light

LUK_8_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_17.text: For there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed, and nothing concealed that will not be made known and brought to light.
LUK_8_17.normalized: for there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed and nothing concealed that will not be made known and brought to light

LUK_8_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_18.text: Pay attention, therefore, to how you listen. Whoever has will be given more, but whoever does not have, even what he thinks he has will be taken away from him.
LUK_8_18.normalized: pay attention therefore to how you listen whoever has will be given more but whoever does not have even what he thinks he has will be taken away from him

LUK_8_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_21.text: My mother and brothers are those who hear the word of God and carry it out.
LUK_8_21.normalized: my mother and brothers are those who hear the word of god and carry it out

LUK_8_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_22.text: Let us cross to the other side of the lake.
LUK_8_22.normalized: let us cross to the other side of the lake

LUK_8_39.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_39.text: Return home and describe how much God has done for you.
LUK_8_39.normalized: return home and describe how much god has done for you

LUK_8_46.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_46.text: Someone touched Me, for I know that power has gone out from Me.
LUK_8_46.normalized: someone touched me for i know that power has gone out from me

LUK_8_48.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_48.text: Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace.
LUK_8_48.normalized: daughter your faith has healed you go in peace

LUK_8_50.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_50.text: Do not be afraid; just believe, and she will be healed.
LUK_8_50.normalized: do not be afraid just believe and she will be healed

LUK_8_52.speaker: Jesus
LUK_8_52.text: Stop weeping; she is not dead but asleep.
LUK_8_52.normalized: stop weeping she is not dead but asleep

LUK_9_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_3.text: Take nothing for the journey, no staff, no bag, no bread, no money, no second tunic.
LUK_9_3.normalized: take nothing for the journey no staff no bag no bread no money no second tunic

LUK_9_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_4.text: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that area.
LUK_9_4.normalized: whatever house you enter stay there until you leave that area

LUK_9_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_5.text: If anyone does not welcome you, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that town, as a testimony against them.
LUK_9_5.normalized: if anyone does not welcome you shake the dust off your feet when you leave that town as a testimony against them

LUK_9_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_13.text: You give them something to eat.
LUK_9_13.normalized: you give them something to eat

LUK_9_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_14.text: Have them sit down in groups of about fifty each.
LUK_9_14.normalized: have them sit down in groups of about fifty each

LUK_9_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_18.text: Who do the crowds say I am?
LUK_9_18.normalized: who do the crowds say i am

LUK_9_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_20.text: But what about you? Who do you say I am?
LUK_9_20.normalized: but what about you who do you say i am

LUK_9_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_22.text: The Son of Man must suffer many things, He must be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and He must be killed and on the third day be raised to life.
LUK_9_22.normalized: the son of man must suffer many things he must be rejected by the elders chief priests and scribes and he must be killed and on the third day be raised to life

LUK_9_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_23.text: If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow Me.
LUK_9_23.normalized: if anyone wants to come after me he must deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me

LUK_9_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_24.text: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will save it.
LUK_9_24.normalized: for whoever wants to save his life will lose it but whoever loses his life for my sake will save it

LUK_9_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_25.text: What does it profit a man to gain the whole world, yet lose or forfeit his very self?
LUK_9_25.normalized: what does it profit a man to gain the whole world yet lose or forfeit his very self

LUK_9_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_26.text: If anyone is ashamed of Me and My words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when He comes in His glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
LUK_9_26.normalized: if anyone is ashamed of me and my words the son of man will be ashamed of him when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the father and of the holy angels

LUK_9_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_27.text: But I tell you truly, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God.
LUK_9_27.normalized: but i tell you truly some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the kingdom of god

LUK_9_41.speaker: Jesus
