LUK_9_41.text: O unbelieving and perverse generation! How long must I remain with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
LUK_9_41.normalized: o unbelieving and perverse generation how long must i remain with you and put up with you bring your son here

LUK_9_44.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_44.text: Let these words sink into your ears: The Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men.
LUK_9_44.normalized: let these words sink into your ears the son of man is about to be delivered into the hands of men

LUK_9_48.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_48.text: Whoever welcomes this little child in My name welcomes Me, and whoever welcomes Me welcomes the One who sent Me. For whoever is the least among all of you, he is the greatest.
LUK_9_48.normalized: whoever welcomes this little child in my name welcomes me and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me for whoever is the least among all of you he is the greatest

LUK_9_50.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_50.text: Do not stop him, for whoever is not against you is for you.
LUK_9_50.normalized: do not stop him for whoever is not against you is for you

LUK_9_58.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_58.text: Foxes have dens and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay His head.
LUK_9_58.normalized: foxes have dens and birds of the air have nests but the son of man has no place to lay his head

LUK_9_60.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_60.text: Let the dead bury their own dead. You, however, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.
LUK_9_60.normalized: let the dead bury their own dead you however go and proclaim the kingdom of god

LUK_9_62.speaker: Jesus
LUK_9_62.text: No one who puts his hand to the plow and then looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.
LUK_9_62.normalized: no one who puts his hand to the plow and then looks back is fit for the kingdom of god

LUK_10_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_2.text: The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.
LUK_10_2.normalized: the harvest is plentiful but the workers are few ask the lord of the harvest therefore to send out workers into his harvest

LUK_10_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_3.text: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.
LUK_10_3.normalized: go i am sending you out like lambs among wolves

LUK_10_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_4.text: Carry no purse or bag or sandals. Do not greet anyone along the road.
LUK_10_4.normalized: carry no purse or bag or sandals do not greet anyone along the road

LUK_10_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_5.text: Whatever house you enter, begin by saying, 'Peace to this house.
LUK_10_5.normalized: whatever house you enter begin by saying peace to this house

LUK_10_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_6.text: If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; if not, it will return to you.
LUK_10_6.normalized: if a man of peace is there your peace will rest on him if not it will return to you

LUK_10_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_7.text: Stay at the same house, eating and drinking whatever you are offered. For the worker is worthy of his wages. Do not move around from house to house.
LUK_10_7.normalized: stay at the same house eating and drinking whatever you are offered for the worker is worthy of his wages do not move around from house to house

LUK_10_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_8.text: If you enter a town and they welcome you, eat whatever is set before you.
LUK_10_8.normalized: if you enter a town and they welcome you eat whatever is set before you

LUK_10_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_9.text: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, 'The kingdom of God is near you.
LUK_10_9.normalized: heal the sick who are there and tell them the kingdom of god is near you

LUK_10_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_10.text: But if you enter a town and they do not welcome you, go into the streets and declare,
LUK_10_10.normalized: but if you enter a town and they do not welcome you go into the streets and declare

LUK_10_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_11.text: Even the dust of your town that clings to our feet, we wipe off as a testimony against you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God is near.
LUK_10_11.normalized: even the dust of your town that clings to our feet we wipe off as a testimony against you yet be sure of this the kingdom of god is near

LUK_10_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_12.text: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.
LUK_10_12.normalized: i tell you it will be more bearable on that day for sodom than for that town

LUK_10_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_13.text: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
LUK_10_13.normalized: woe to you chorazin woe to you bethsaida for if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in tyre and sidon they would have repented long ago sitting in sackcloth and ashes

LUK_10_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_14.text: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
LUK_10_14.normalized: but it will be more bearable for tyre and sidon at the judgment than for you

LUK_10_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_15.text: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades!
LUK_10_15.normalized: and you capernaum will you be lifted up to heaven no you will be brought down to hades

LUK_10_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_16.text: Whoever listens to you listens to Me; whoever rejects you rejects Me; and whoever rejects Me rejects the One who sent Me.
LUK_10_16.normalized: whoever listens to you listens to me whoever rejects you rejects me and whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me

LUK_10_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_18.text: I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
LUK_10_18.normalized: i saw satan fall like lightning from heaven

LUK_10_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_19.text: Behold, I have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
LUK_10_19.normalized: behold i have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions and over all the power of the enemy nothing will harm you

LUK_10_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_20.text: Nevertheless, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.
LUK_10_20.normalized: nevertheless do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you but rejoice that your names are written in heaven

LUK_10_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_21.text: I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.
LUK_10_21.normalized: i praise you father lord of heaven and earth because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned and revealed them to little children yes father for this was well pleasing in your sight

LUK_10_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_22.text: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.
LUK_10_22.normalized: all things have been entrusted to me by my father no one knows who the son is except the father and no one knows who the father is except the son and those to whom the son chooses to reveal him

LUK_10_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_23.text: Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.
LUK_10_23.normalized: blessed are the eyes that see what you see

LUK_10_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_24.text: For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.
LUK_10_24.normalized: for i tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see but did not see it and to hear what you hear but did not hear it

LUK_10_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_26.text: What is written in the Law? How do you read it?
LUK_10_26.normalized: what is written in the law how do you read it

LUK_10_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_28.text: You have answered correctly, Do this and you will live.
LUK_10_28.normalized: you have answered correctly do this and you will live

LUK_10_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_30.text: A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho when he fell into the hands of robbers. They stripped him, beat him, and went away, leaving him half dead.
LUK_10_30.normalized: a man was going down from jerusalem to jericho when he fell into the hands of robbers they stripped him beat him and went away leaving him half dead

LUK_10_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_31.text: Now by chance a priest was going down the same road, but when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
LUK_10_31.normalized: now by chance a priest was going down the same road but when he saw him he passed by on the other side

LUK_10_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_32.text: So too, when a Levite came to that spot and saw him, he passed by on the other side.
LUK_10_32.normalized: so too when a levite came to that spot and saw him he passed by on the other side

LUK_10_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_33.text: But a Samaritan on a journey came upon him, and when he saw him, he had compassion.
LUK_10_33.normalized: but a samaritan on a journey came upon him and when he saw him he had compassion

LUK_10_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_34.text: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
LUK_10_34.normalized: he went to him and bandaged his wounds pouring on oil and wine then he put him on his own animal brought him to an inn and took care of him

LUK_10_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_35.text: The next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper. 'Take care of him,' he said, 'and on my return I will repay you for any additional expense.
LUK_10_35.normalized: the next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper take care of him he said and on my return i will repay you for any additional expense

LUK_10_36.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_36.text: Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?
LUK_10_36.normalized: which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers

LUK_10_41.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_41.text: Martha, Martha, you are worried and upset about many things.
LUK_10_41.normalized: martha martha you are worried and upset about many things

LUK_10_42.speaker: Jesus
LUK_10_42.text: But only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, and it will not be taken away from her.
LUK_10_42.normalized: but only one thing is necessary mary has chosen the good portion and it will not be taken away from her

LUK_11_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_2.text: When you pray, say: 'Father, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come.
LUK_11_2.normalized: when you pray say father hallowed be your name your kingdom come

LUK_11_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_3.text: Give us each day our daily bread.
LUK_11_3.normalized: give us each day our daily bread

LUK_11_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_4.text: And forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who sins against us. And lead us not into temptation.
LUK_11_4.normalized: and forgive us our sins for we also forgive everyone who sins against us and lead us not into temptation

LUK_11_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_5.text: Suppose one of you goes to his friend at midnight and says, 'Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,
LUK_11_5.normalized: suppose one of you goes to his friend at midnight and says friend lend me three loaves of bread

LUK_11_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_6.text: because a friend of mine has come to me on a journey, and I have nothing to set before him.
LUK_11_6.normalized: because a friend of mine has come to me on a journey and i have nothing to set before him

LUK_11_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_7.text: And suppose the one inside answers, 'Do not bother me. My door is already shut, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you anything.
LUK_11_7.normalized: and suppose the one inside answers do not bother me my door is already shut and my children and i are in bed i cannot get up to give you anything

LUK_11_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_8.text: I tell you, even though he will not get up to provide for him because of his friendship, yet because of the man's persistence, he will get up and give him as much as he needs.
LUK_11_8.normalized: i tell you even though he will not get up to provide for him because of his friendship yet because of the man s persistence he will get up and give him as much as he needs

LUK_11_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_9.text: So I tell you: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.
LUK_11_9.normalized: so i tell you ask and it will be given to you seek and you will find knock and the door will be opened to you

LUK_11_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_10.text: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.
LUK_11_10.normalized: for everyone who asks receives he who seeks finds and to him who knocks the door will be opened

LUK_11_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_11.text: What father among you, if his son asks for a fish, will give him a snake instead?
LUK_11_11.normalized: what father among you if his son asks for a fish will give him a snake instead

LUK_11_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_12.text: Or if he asks for an egg, will give him a scorpion?
LUK_11_12.normalized: or if he asks for an egg will give him a scorpion

LUK_11_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_13.text: So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him!
LUK_11_13.normalized: so if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children how much more will your father in heaven give the holy spirit to those who ask him

LUK_11_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_17.text: Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and a house divided against a house will fall.
LUK_11_17.normalized: every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste and a house divided against a house will fall

LUK_11_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_18.text: If Satan is divided against himself, how can his kingdom stand? After all, you say that I drive out demons by Beelzebul.
LUK_11_18.normalized: if satan is divided against himself how can his kingdom stand after all you say that i drive out demons by beelzebul

LUK_11_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_19.text: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.
LUK_11_19.normalized: and if i drive out demons by beelzebul by whom do your sons drive them out so then they will be your judges

LUK_11_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_20.text: But if I drive out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
LUK_11_20.normalized: but if i drive out demons by the finger of god then the kingdom of god has come upon you

LUK_11_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_21.text: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his house, his possessions are secure.
LUK_11_21.normalized: when a strong man fully armed guards his house his possessions are secure

LUK_11_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_22.text: But when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him, he takes away the armor in which the man trusted, and then he divides up his plunder.
LUK_11_22.normalized: but when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him he takes away the armor in which the man trusted and then he divides up his plunder

LUK_11_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_23.text: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.
LUK_11_23.normalized: he who is not with me is against me and he who does not gather with me scatters

LUK_11_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_24.text: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it. Then it says, 'I will return to the house I left.
LUK_11_24.normalized: when an unclean spirit comes out of a man it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it then it says i will return to the house i left

LUK_11_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_25.text: On its return, it finds the house swept clean and put in order.
LUK_11_25.normalized: on its return it finds the house swept clean and put in order

LUK_11_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_26.text: Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there. And the final plight of that man is worse than the first.
LUK_11_26.normalized: then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself and they go in and dwell there and the final plight of that man is worse than the first

LUK_11_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_28.text: Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it.
LUK_11_28.normalized: blessed rather are those who hear the word of god and obey it

LUK_11_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_29.text: This is a wicked generation. It demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.
LUK_11_29.normalized: this is a wicked generation it demands a sign but none will be given it except the sign of jonah

LUK_11_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_30.text: For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will be a sign to this generation.
LUK_11_30.normalized: for as jonah was a sign to the ninevites so the son of man will be a sign to this generation

LUK_11_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_31.text: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.
LUK_11_31.normalized: the queen of the south will rise at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of solomon and now one greater than solomon is here

LUK_11_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_32.text: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.
LUK_11_32.normalized: the men of nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it for they repented at the preaching of jonah and now one greater than jonah is here

LUK_11_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_33.text: No one lights a lamp and puts it in a cellar or under a basket. Instead, he sets it on a stand, so those who enter can see the light.
LUK_11_33.normalized: no one lights a lamp and puts it in a cellar or under a basket instead he sets it on a stand so those who enter can see the light

LUK_11_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_34.text: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eyes are good, your whole body also is full of light. But when they are bad, your body is full of darkness.
LUK_11_34.normalized: your eye is the lamp of your body when your eyes are good your whole body also is full of light but when they are bad your body is full of darkness

LUK_11_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_35.text: Be careful, then, that the light within you is not darkness.
LUK_11_35.normalized: be careful then that the light within you is not darkness

LUK_11_36.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_36.text: So if your whole body is full of light, with no part of it in darkness, you will be radiant, as though a lamp were shining on you.
LUK_11_36.normalized: so if your whole body is full of light with no part of it in darkness you will be radiant as though a lamp were shining on you

LUK_11_39.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_39.text: Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness.
LUK_11_39.normalized: now you pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish but inside you are full of greed and wickedness

LUK_11_40.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_40.text: You fools! Did not the One who made the outside make the inside as well?
LUK_11_40.normalized: you fools did not the one who made the outside make the inside as well

LUK_11_41.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_41.text: But give as alms the things that are within you, and behold, everything will be clean for you.
LUK_11_41.normalized: but give as alms the things that are within you and behold everything will be clean for you

LUK_11_42.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_42.text: Woe to you Pharisees! For you pay tithes of mint, rue, and every herb, but you disregard justice and the love of God. You should have practiced the latter without neglecting the former.
LUK_11_42.normalized: woe to you pharisees for you pay tithes of mint rue and every herb but you disregard justice and the love of god you should have practiced the latter without neglecting the former

LUK_11_43.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_43.text: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the chief seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces.
LUK_11_43.normalized: woe to you pharisees for you love the chief seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces

LUK_11_44.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_44.text: Woe to you! For you are like unmarked graves, which men walk over without even noticing.
LUK_11_44.normalized: woe to you for you are like unmarked graves which men walk over without even noticing

LUK_11_46.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_46.text: Woe to you as well, experts in the law! For you weigh men down with heavy burdens, but you yourselves will not lift a finger to lighten their load.
LUK_11_46.normalized: woe to you as well experts in the law for you weigh men down with heavy burdens but you yourselves will not lift a finger to lighten their load

LUK_11_47.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_47.text: Woe to you! For you build tombs for the prophets, but it was your fathers who killed them.
LUK_11_47.normalized: woe to you for you build tombs for the prophets but it was your fathers who killed them

LUK_11_48.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_48.text: So you are witnesses consenting to the deeds of your fathers: They killed the prophets, and you build their tombs.
LUK_11_48.normalized: so you are witnesses consenting to the deeds of your fathers they killed the prophets and you build their tombs

LUK_11_49.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_49.text: Because of this, the wisdom of God said, 'I will send them prophets and apostles; some of them they will kill and others they will persecute.
LUK_11_49.normalized: because of this the wisdom of god said i will send them prophets and apostles some of them they will kill and others they will persecute

LUK_11_50.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_50.text: As a result, this generation will be charged with the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the foundation of the world,
LUK_11_50.normalized: as a result this generation will be charged with the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the foundation of the world

LUK_11_51.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_51.text: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, all of it will be charged to this generation.
LUK_11_51.normalized: from the blood of abel to the blood of zechariah who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary yes i tell you all of it will be charged to this generation

LUK_11_52.speaker: Jesus
LUK_11_52.text: Woe to you experts in the law! For you have taken away the key to knowledge. You yourselves have not entered, and you have hindered those who were entering.
LUK_11_52.normalized: woe to you experts in the law for you have taken away the key to knowledge you yourselves have not entered and you have hindered those who were entering

LUK_12_1.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_1.text: Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
LUK_12_1.normalized: beware of the leaven of the pharisees which is hypocrisy

LUK_12_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_2.text: There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.
LUK_12_2.normalized: there is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed and nothing hidden that will not be made known

LUK_12_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_3.text: What you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the daylight, and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops.
LUK_12_3.normalized: what you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the daylight and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops

LUK_12_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_4.text: I tell you, My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more.
LUK_12_4.normalized: i tell you my friends do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more

LUK_12_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_5.text: But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear the One who, after you have been killed, has authority to throw you into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear Him!
LUK_12_5.normalized: but i will show you whom you should fear fear the one who after you have been killed has authority to throw you into hell yes i tell you fear him

LUK_12_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_6.text: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God.
LUK_12_6.normalized: are not five sparrows sold for two pennies yet not one of them is forgotten by god

LUK_12_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_7.text: And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
LUK_12_7.normalized: and even the very hairs of your head are all numbered so do not be afraid you are worth more than many sparrows

LUK_12_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_8.text: I tell you, everyone who confesses Me before men, the Son of Man will also confess him before the angels of God.
LUK_12_8.normalized: i tell you everyone who confesses me before men the son of man will also confess him before the angels of god

LUK_12_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_9.text: But whoever denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.
LUK_12_9.normalized: but whoever denies me before men will be denied before the angels of god

LUK_12_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_10.text: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
LUK_12_10.normalized: and everyone who speaks a word against the son of man will be forgiven but whoever blasphemes against the holy spirit will not be forgiven

LUK_12_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_11.text: When you are brought before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, do not worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say.
LUK_12_11.normalized: when you are brought before synagogues rulers and authorities do not worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say

LUK_12_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_12.text: For at that time the Holy Spirit will teach you what you should say.
LUK_12_12.normalized: for at that time the holy spirit will teach you what you should say

LUK_12_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_14.text: Man, who appointed Me judge or executor between you?
LUK_12_14.normalized: man who appointed me judge or executor between you

LUK_12_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_15.text: Watch out! Guard yourselves against every form of greed, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.
LUK_12_15.normalized: watch out guard yourselves against every form of greed for one s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions

LUK_12_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_16.text: The ground of a certain rich man produced an abundance.
LUK_12_16.normalized: the ground of a certain rich man produced an abundance

LUK_12_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_17.text: So he thought to himself, 'What shall I do, since I have nowhere to store my crops?
LUK_12_17.normalized: so he thought to himself what shall i do since i have nowhere to store my crops

LUK_12_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_18.text: Then he said, 'This is what I will do: I will tear down my barns and will build bigger ones, and there I will store up all my grain and my goods.
LUK_12_18.normalized: then he said this is what i will do i will tear down my barns and will build bigger ones and there i will store up all my grain and my goods

LUK_12_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_19.text: Then I will say to myself, "You have plenty of good things laid up for many years. Take it easy. Eat, drink, and be merry!
LUK_12_19.normalized: then i will say to myself you have plenty of good things laid up for many years take it easy eat drink and be merry

LUK_12_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_20.text: But God said to him, 'You fool! This very night your life will be required of you. Then who will own what you have accumulated?
LUK_12_20.normalized: but god said to him you fool this very night your life will be required of you then who will own what you have accumulated

LUK_12_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_21.text: This is how it will be for anyone who stores up treasure for himself but is not rich toward God.
LUK_12_21.normalized: this is how it will be for anyone who stores up treasure for himself but is not rich toward god

LUK_12_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_22.text: Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
LUK_12_22.normalized: therefore i tell you do not worry about your life what you will eat or about your body what you will wear

LUK_12_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_23.text: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothes.
LUK_12_23.normalized: for life is more than food and the body more than clothes

LUK_12_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_24.text: Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storehouse or barn; yet God feeds them. How much more valuable you are than the birds!
LUK_12_24.normalized: consider the ravens they do not sow or reap they have no storehouse or barn yet god feeds them how much more valuable you are than the birds

LUK_12_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_25.text: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?
LUK_12_25.normalized: who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life

LUK_12_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_26.text: So if you cannot do such a small thing, why do you worry about the rest?
LUK_12_26.normalized: so if you cannot do such a small thing why do you worry about the rest

LUK_12_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_27.text: Consider how the lilies grow: They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these.
LUK_12_27.normalized: consider how the lilies grow they do not labor or spin yet i tell you not even solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these

LUK_12_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_28.text: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, how much more will He clothe you, O you of little faith!
LUK_12_28.normalized: if that is how god clothes the grass of the field which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace how much more will he clothe you o you of little faith

LUK_12_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_29.text: And do not be concerned about what you will eat or drink. Do not worry about it.
LUK_12_29.normalized: and do not be concerned about what you will eat or drink do not worry about it

LUK_12_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_30.text: For the Gentiles of the world strive after all these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
LUK_12_30.normalized: for the gentiles of the world strive after all these things and your father knows that you need them

LUK_12_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_31.text: But seek His kingdom, and these things will be added unto you.
LUK_12_31.normalized: but seek his kingdom and these things will be added unto you

LUK_12_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_32.text: Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father is pleased to give you the kingdom.
LUK_12_32.normalized: do not be afraid little flock for your father is pleased to give you the kingdom

LUK_12_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_33.text: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide yourselves with purses that will not wear out, an inexhaustible treasure in heaven, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.
LUK_12_33.normalized: sell your possessions and give to the poor provide yourselves with purses that will not wear out an inexhaustible treasure in heaven where no thief approaches and no moth destroys

LUK_12_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_34.text: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
LUK_12_34.normalized: for where your treasure is there your heart will be also

LUK_12_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_35.text: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps burning.
LUK_12_35.normalized: be dressed for service and keep your lamps burning

LUK_12_36.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_36.text: Then you will be like servants waiting for their master to return from the wedding banquet, so that when he comes and knocks, they can open the door for him at once.
LUK_12_36.normalized: then you will be like servants waiting for their master to return from the wedding banquet so that when he comes and knocks they can open the door for him at once

LUK_12_37.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_37.text: Blessed are those servants whom the master finds on watch when he returns. Truly I tell you, he will dress himself to serve and will have them recline at the table, and he himself will come and wait on them.
LUK_12_37.normalized: blessed are those servants whom the master finds on watch when he returns truly i tell you he will dress himself to serve and will have them recline at the table and he himself will come and wait on them

LUK_12_38.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_38.text: Even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night and finds them alert, those servants will be blessed.
LUK_12_38.normalized: even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night and finds them alert those servants will be blessed

LUK_12_39.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_39.text: But understand this: If the homeowner had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
LUK_12_39.normalized: but understand this if the homeowner had known at what hour the thief was coming he would not have let his house be broken into

LUK_12_40.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_40.text: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you do not expect.
LUK_12_40.normalized: you also must be ready because the son of man will come at an hour you do not expect

LUK_12_42.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_42.text: Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their portion at the proper time?
LUK_12_42.normalized: who then is the faithful and wise manager whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their portion at the proper time

LUK_12_43.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_43.text: Blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns.
LUK_12_43.normalized: blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns

LUK_12_44.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_44.text: Truly I tell you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
LUK_12_44.normalized: truly i tell you he will put him in charge of all his possessions

LUK_12_45.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_45.text: But suppose that servant says in his heart, 'My master will be a long time in coming,' and he begins to beat the menservants and maidservants, and to eat and drink and get drunk.
LUK_12_45.normalized: but suppose that servant says in his heart my master will be a long time in coming and he begins to beat the menservants and maidservants and to eat and drink and get drunk

LUK_12_46.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_46.text: The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate. Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
LUK_12_46.normalized: the master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers

LUK_12_47.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_47.text: That servant who knows his master's will but does not get ready or follow his instructions will be beaten with many blows.
LUK_12_47.normalized: that servant who knows his master s will but does not get ready or follow his instructions will be beaten with many blows

LUK_12_48.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_48.text: But the one who unknowingly does things worthy of punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required; and from him who has been entrusted with much, even more will be demanded.
LUK_12_48.normalized: but the one who unknowingly does things worthy of punishment will be beaten with few blows from everyone who has been given much much will be required and from him who has been entrusted with much even more will be demanded

LUK_12_49.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_49.text: I have come to ignite a fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!
LUK_12_49.normalized: i have come to ignite a fire on the earth and how i wish it were already kindled

LUK_12_50.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_50.text: But I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!
LUK_12_50.normalized: but i have a baptism to undergo and how distressed i am until it is accomplished

LUK_12_51.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_51.text: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, but division.
LUK_12_51.normalized: do you think that i have come to bring peace to the earth no i tell you but division

LUK_12_52.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_52.text: From now on, five in one household will be divided, three against two and two against three.
LUK_12_52.normalized: from now on five in one household will be divided three against two and two against three

LUK_12_53.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_53.text: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
LUK_12_53.normalized: they will be divided father against son and son against father mother against daughter and daughter against mother mother in law against daughter in law and daughter in law against mother in law

LUK_12_54.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_54.text: As soon as you see a cloud rising in the west, you say, 'A shower is coming,' and that is what happens.
LUK_12_54.normalized: as soon as you see a cloud rising in the west you say a shower is coming and that is what happens

LUK_12_55.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_55.text: And when the south wind blows, you say, 'It will be hot,' and it is.
LUK_12_55.normalized: and when the south wind blows you say it will be hot and it is

LUK_12_56.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_56.text: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and sky. Why don't you know how to interpret the present time?
LUK_12_56.normalized: you hypocrites you know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and sky why don t you know how to interpret the present time

LUK_12_57.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_57.text: And why don't you judge for yourselves what is right?
LUK_12_57.normalized: and why don t you judge for yourselves what is right

LUK_12_58.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_58.text: Make every effort to reconcile with your adversary while you are on your way to the magistrate. Otherwise, he may drag you off to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and the officer may throw you into prison.
LUK_12_58.normalized: make every effort to reconcile with your adversary while you are on your way to the magistrate otherwise he may drag you off to the judge and the judge may hand you over to the officer and the officer may throw you into prison

LUK_12_59.speaker: Jesus
LUK_12_59.text: I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the very last penny.
LUK_12_59.normalized: i tell you you will not get out until you have paid the very last penny

LUK_13_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_2.text: Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered this way?
LUK_13_2.normalized: do you think that these galileans were worse sinners than all the other galileans because they suffered this way

LUK_13_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_3.text: No, I tell you. But unless you repent, you too will all perish.
LUK_13_3.normalized: no i tell you but unless you repent you too will all perish

LUK_13_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_4.text: Or those eighteen who were killed when the tower of Siloam collapsed on them: Do you think that they were more sinful than all the others living in Jerusalem?
LUK_13_4.normalized: or those eighteen who were killed when the tower of siloam collapsed on them do you think that they were more sinful than all the others living in jerusalem

LUK_13_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_5.text: No, I tell you. But unless you repent, you too will all perish.
LUK_13_5.normalized: no i tell you but unless you repent you too will all perish

LUK_13_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_6.text: A man had a fig tree that was planted in his vineyard. He went to look for fruit on it but did not find any.
LUK_13_6.normalized: a man had a fig tree that was planted in his vineyard he went to look for fruit on it but did not find any

LUK_13_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_7.text: So he said to the keeper of the vineyard, 'Look, for the past three years I have come to search for fruit on this fig tree and haven't found any. Therefore cut it down! Why should it use up the soil?
LUK_13_7.normalized: so he said to the keeper of the vineyard look for the past three years i have come to search for fruit on this fig tree and haven t found any therefore cut it down why should it use up the soil

LUK_13_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_8.text: Sir,' the man replied, 'leave it alone again this year, until I dig around it and fertilize it.
LUK_13_8.normalized: sir the man replied leave it alone again this year until i dig around it and fertilize it

LUK_13_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_9.text: If it bears fruit next year, fine. But if not, you can cut it down.
LUK_13_9.normalized: if it bears fruit next year fine but if not you can cut it down

LUK_13_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_12.text: Woman, you are set free from your disability.
LUK_13_12.normalized: woman you are set free from your disability

LUK_13_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_15.text: You hypocrites! Does not each of you on the Sabbath untie his ox or donkey from the stall and lead it to water?
LUK_13_15.normalized: you hypocrites does not each of you on the sabbath untie his ox or donkey from the stall and lead it to water

LUK_13_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_16.text: Then should not this daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has kept bound for eighteen long years, be released from her bondage on the Sabbath day?
LUK_13_16.normalized: then should not this daughter of abraham whom satan has kept bound for eighteen long years be released from her bondage on the sabbath day

LUK_13_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_18.text: What is the kingdom of God like? To what can I compare it?
LUK_13_18.normalized: what is the kingdom of god like to what can i compare it

LUK_13_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_19.text: It is like a mustard seed that a man tossed into his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the birds of the air nested in its branches.
LUK_13_19.normalized: it is like a mustard seed that a man tossed into his garden it grew and became a tree and the birds of the air nested in its branches

LUK_13_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_20.text: To what can I compare the kingdom of God?
LUK_13_20.normalized: to what can i compare the kingdom of god

LUK_13_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_21.text: It is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.
LUK_13_21.normalized: it is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour until all of it was leavened

LUK_13_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_24.text: Make every effort to enter through the narrow door. For many, I tell you, will try to enter and will not be able.
LUK_13_24.normalized: make every effort to enter through the narrow door for many i tell you will try to enter and will not be able

LUK_13_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_25.text: After the master of the house gets up and shuts the door, you will stand outside knocking and saying, 'Lord, open the door for us.'But he will reply, 'I do not know where you are from.
LUK_13_25.normalized: after the master of the house gets up and shuts the door you will stand outside knocking and saying lord open the door for us but he will reply i do not know where you are from

LUK_13_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_26.text: Then you will say, 'We ate and drank with you, and you taught in our streets.
LUK_13_26.normalized: then you will say we ate and drank with you and you taught in our streets

LUK_13_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_27.text: And he will answer, 'I tell you, I do not know where you are from. Depart from me, all you evildoers.
LUK_13_27.normalized: and he will answer i tell you i do not know where you are from depart from me all you evildoers

LUK_13_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_28.text: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but you yourselves are thrown out.
LUK_13_28.normalized: there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see abraham isaac jacob and all the prophets in the kingdom of god but you yourselves are thrown out

LUK_13_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_29.text: People will come from east and west and north and south, and will recline at the table in the kingdom of God.
LUK_13_29.normalized: people will come from east and west and north and south and will recline at the table in the kingdom of god

LUK_13_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_30.text: And indeed, some who are last will be first, and some who are first will be last.
LUK_13_30.normalized: and indeed some who are last will be first and some who are first will be last

LUK_13_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_32.text: Go tell that fox, 'Look, I will keep driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will reach My goal.
LUK_13_32.normalized: go tell that fox look i will keep driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow and on the third day i will reach my goal

LUK_13_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_33.text: Nevertheless, I must keep going today and tomorrow and the next day, for it is not admissible for a prophet to perish outside of Jerusalem.
LUK_13_33.normalized: nevertheless i must keep going today and tomorrow and the next day for it is not admissible for a prophet to perish outside of jerusalem

LUK_13_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_34.text: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her, how often I have longed to gather your children together as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were unwilling!
LUK_13_34.normalized: o jerusalem jerusalem who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her how often i have longed to gather your children together as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings but you were unwilling

LUK_13_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_13_35.text: Look, your house is left to you desolate. And I tell you that you will not see Me again until you say, 'Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord.
LUK_13_35.normalized: look your house is left to you desolate and i tell you that you will not see me again until you say blessed is he who comes in the name of the lord

LUK_14_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_3.text: Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath or not?
LUK_14_3.normalized: is it lawful to heal on the sabbath or not

LUK_14_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_5.text: Which of you whose son or ox falls into a pit on the Sabbath day will not immediately pull him out?
LUK_14_5.normalized: which of you whose son or ox falls into a pit on the sabbath day will not immediately pull him out

LUK_14_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_8.text: When you are invited to a wedding banquet, do not sit in the place of honor, in case someone more distinguished than you has been invited.
LUK_14_8.normalized: when you are invited to a wedding banquet do not sit in the place of honor in case someone more distinguished than you has been invited

LUK_14_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_9.text: Then the host who invited both of you will come and tell you, 'Give this man your seat.' And in humiliation, you will have to take the last place.
LUK_14_9.normalized: then the host who invited both of you will come and tell you give this man your seat and in humiliation you will have to take the last place

LUK_14_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_10.text: But when you are invited, go and sit in the last place, so that your host will come and tell you, 'Friend, move up to a better place.' Then you will be honored in front of everyone at the table with you.
LUK_14_10.normalized: but when you are invited go and sit in the last place so that your host will come and tell you friend move up to a better place then you will be honored in front of everyone at the table with you

LUK_14_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_11.text: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and the one who humbles himself will be exalted.
LUK_14_11.normalized: for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled and the one who humbles himself will be exalted

LUK_14_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_12.text: When you host a dinner or a banquet, do not invite your friends or brothers or relatives or rich neighbors. Otherwise, they may invite you in return, and you will be repaid.
LUK_14_12.normalized: when you host a dinner or a banquet do not invite your friends or brothers or relatives or rich neighbors otherwise they may invite you in return and you will be repaid

LUK_14_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_13.text: But when you host a banquet, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, and the blind,
LUK_14_13.normalized: but when you host a banquet invite the poor the crippled the lame and the blind

LUK_14_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_14.text: and you will be blessed. Since they cannot repay you, you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.
LUK_14_14.normalized: and you will be blessed since they cannot repay you you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous

LUK_14_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_16.text: A certain man prepared a great banquet and invited many guests.
LUK_14_16.normalized: a certain man prepared a great banquet and invited many guests

LUK_14_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_17.text: When it was time for the banquet, he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited, 'Come, for everything is now ready.
LUK_14_17.normalized: when it was time for the banquet he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited come for everything is now ready

LUK_14_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_18.text: But one after another they all began to make excuses. The first one said, 'I have bought a field, and I need to go see it. Please excuse me.
LUK_14_18.normalized: but one after another they all began to make excuses the first one said i have bought a field and i need to go see it please excuse me

LUK_14_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_19.text: Another said, 'I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to try them out. Please excuse me.
LUK_14_19.normalized: another said i have bought five yoke of oxen and i am going to try them out please excuse me

LUK_14_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_20.text: Still another said, 'I have married a wife, so I cannot come.
LUK_14_20.normalized: still another said i have married a wife so i cannot come

LUK_14_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_21.text: The servant returned and reported all this to his master. Then the owner of the house became angry and said to his servant, 'Go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the city, and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.
LUK_14_21.normalized: the servant returned and reported all this to his master then the owner of the house became angry and said to his servant go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the city and bring in the poor the crippled the blind and the lame

LUK_14_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_22.text: Sir,' the servant replied, 'what you ordered has been done, and there is still room.
LUK_14_22.normalized: sir the servant replied what you ordered has been done and there is still room

LUK_14_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_23.text: So the master told his servant, 'Go out to the highways and hedges and compel them to come in, so that my house will be full.
LUK_14_23.normalized: so the master told his servant go out to the highways and hedges and compel them to come in so that my house will be full

LUK_14_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_24.text: For I tell you, not one of those men who were invited will taste my banquet.
LUK_14_24.normalized: for i tell you not one of those men who were invited will taste my banquet

LUK_14_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_26.text: If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters-yes, even his own life-he cannot be My disciple.
LUK_14_26.normalized: if anyone comes to me and does not hate his father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters yes even his own life he cannot be my disciple

LUK_14_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_27.text: And whoever does not carry his cross and follow Me cannot be My disciple.
LUK_14_27.normalized: and whoever does not carry his cross and follow me cannot be my disciple

LUK_14_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_28.text: Which of you, wishing to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost to see if he has the resources to complete it?
LUK_14_28.normalized: which of you wishing to build a tower does not first sit down and count the cost to see if he has the resources to complete it

LUK_14_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_29.text: Otherwise, if he lays the foundation and is unable to finish the work, everyone who sees it will ridicule him,
LUK_14_29.normalized: otherwise if he lays the foundation and is unable to finish the work everyone who sees it will ridicule him

LUK_14_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_30.text: saying, 'This man could not finish what he started to build.
LUK_14_30.normalized: saying this man could not finish what he started to build

LUK_14_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_31.text: Or what king on his way to war with another king will not first sit down and consider whether he can engage with ten thousand men the one coming against him with twenty thousand?
LUK_14_31.normalized: or what king on his way to war with another king will not first sit down and consider whether he can engage with ten thousand men the one coming against him with twenty thousand

LUK_14_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_32.text: And if he is unable, he will send a delegation while the other king is still far off, to ask for terms of peace.
LUK_14_32.normalized: and if he is unable he will send a delegation while the other king is still far off to ask for terms of peace

LUK_14_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_33.text: In the same way, any one of you who does not give up everything he has cannot be My disciple.
LUK_14_33.normalized: in the same way any one of you who does not give up everything he has cannot be my disciple

LUK_14_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_34.text: Salt is good, but if the salt loses its savor, with what will it be seasoned?
LUK_14_34.normalized: salt is good but if the salt loses its savor with what will it be seasoned

LUK_14_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_14_35.text: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile, and it is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
LUK_14_35.normalized: it is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile and it is thrown out he who has ears to hear let him hear

LUK_15_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_4.text: What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the pasture and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?
LUK_15_4.normalized: what man among you if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them does not leave the ninety nine in the pasture and go after the one that is lost until he finds it

LUK_15_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_5.text: And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders,
LUK_15_5.normalized: and when he finds it he joyfully puts it on his shoulders

LUK_15_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_6.text: comes home, and calls together his friends and neighbors to tell them, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost sheep!
LUK_15_6.normalized: comes home and calls together his friends and neighbors to tell them rejoice with me for i have found my lost sheep

LUK_15_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_7.text: I tell you that in the same way there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous ones who do not need to repent.
LUK_15_7.normalized: i tell you that in the same way there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety nine righteous ones who do not need to repent

LUK_15_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_8.text: Or what woman who has ten silver coins and loses one of them does not light a lamp, sweep her house, and search carefully until she finds it?
LUK_15_8.normalized: or what woman who has ten silver coins and loses one of them does not light a lamp sweep her house and search carefully until she finds it

LUK_15_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_9.text: And when she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors to say, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost coin.
LUK_15_9.normalized: and when she finds it she calls together her friends and neighbors to say rejoice with me for i have found my lost coin

LUK_15_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_10.text: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of God's angels over one sinner who repents.
LUK_15_10.normalized: in the same way i tell you there is joy in the presence of god s angels over one sinner who repents

LUK_15_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_11.text: There was a man who had two sons.
LUK_15_11.normalized: there was a man who had two sons

LUK_15_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_12.text: The younger son said to him, 'Father, give me my share of the estate.' So he divided his property between them.
LUK_15_12.normalized: the younger son said to him father give me my share of the estate so he divided his property between them

LUK_15_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_13.text: After a few days, the younger son got everything together and journeyed to a distant country, where he squandered his wealth in wild living.
LUK_15_13.normalized: after a few days the younger son got everything together and journeyed to a distant country where he squandered his wealth in wild living

LUK_15_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_14.text: After he had spent all he had, a severe famine swept through that country, and he began to be in need.
LUK_15_14.normalized: after he had spent all he had a severe famine swept through that country and he began to be in need

LUK_15_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_15.text: So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.
LUK_15_15.normalized: so he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country who sent him into his fields to feed the pigs

LUK_15_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_16.text: He longed to fill his belly with the pods the pigs were eating, but no one would give him a thing.
LUK_15_16.normalized: he longed to fill his belly with the pods the pigs were eating but no one would give him a thing

LUK_15_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_17.text: Finally he came to his senses and said, 'How many of my father's hired servants have plenty of food, but here I am, starving to death!
LUK_15_17.normalized: finally he came to his senses and said how many of my father s hired servants have plenty of food but here i am starving to death

LUK_15_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_18.text: I will get up and go back to my father and say to him, "Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
LUK_15_18.normalized: i will get up and go back to my father and say to him father i have sinned against heaven and against you

LUK_15_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_19.text: I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Make me like one of your hired servants.
LUK_15_19.normalized: i am no longer worthy to be called your son make me like one of your hired servants

LUK_15_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_20.text: So he got up and went to his father. But while he was still in the distance, his father saw him and was filled with compassion. He ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.
LUK_15_20.normalized: so he got up and went to his father but while he was still in the distance his father saw him and was filled with compassion he ran to his son embraced him and kissed him

LUK_15_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_21.text: The son declared, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.
LUK_15_21.normalized: the son declared father i have sinned against heaven and against you i am no longer worthy to be called your son

LUK_15_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_22.text: But the father said to his servants, 'Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
LUK_15_22.normalized: but the father said to his servants quick bring the best robe and put it on him put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet

LUK_15_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_23.text: Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let us feast and celebrate.
LUK_15_23.normalized: bring the fattened calf and kill it let us feast and celebrate

LUK_15_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_24.text: For this son of mine was dead and is alive again! He was lost and is found!' So they began to celebrate.
LUK_15_24.normalized: for this son of mine was dead and is alive again he was lost and is found so they began to celebrate

LUK_15_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_25.text: Meanwhile the older son was in the field, and as he approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
LUK_15_25.normalized: meanwhile the older son was in the field and as he approached the house he heard music and dancing

LUK_15_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_26.text: So he called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
LUK_15_26.normalized: so he called one of the servants and asked what was going on

LUK_15_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_27.text: Your brother has returned,' he said, 'and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has him back safe and sound.
LUK_15_27.normalized: your brother has returned he said and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has him back safe and sound

LUK_15_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_28.text: The older son became angry and refused to go in. So his father came out and pleaded with him.
LUK_15_28.normalized: the older son became angry and refused to go in so his father came out and pleaded with him

LUK_15_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_29.text: But he answered his father, 'Look, all these years I have served you and never disobeyed a commandment of yours. Yet you never gave me even a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
LUK_15_29.normalized: but he answered his father look all these years i have served you and never disobeyed a commandment of yours yet you never gave me even a young goat so i could celebrate with my friends

LUK_15_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_30.text: But when this son of yours returns from squandering your wealth with prostitutes, you kill the fattened calf for him!
LUK_15_30.normalized: but when this son of yours returns from squandering your wealth with prostitutes you kill the fattened calf for him

LUK_15_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_31.text: Son, you are always with me,' the father said, 'and all that is mine is yours.
LUK_15_31.normalized: son you are always with me the father said and all that is mine is yours

LUK_15_32.speaker: Jesus
LUK_15_32.text: But it was fitting to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.
LUK_15_32.normalized: but it was fitting to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again he was lost and is found

LUK_16_1.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_1.text: There was a rich man whose manager was accused of wasting his possessions.
LUK_16_1.normalized: there was a rich man whose manager was accused of wasting his possessions

LUK_16_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_2.text: So he called him in to ask, 'What is this I hear about you? Turn in an account of your management, for you cannot be manager any longer.
LUK_16_2.normalized: so he called him in to ask what is this i hear about you turn in an account of your management for you cannot be manager any longer

LUK_16_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_3.text: The manager said to himself, 'What shall I do, now that my master is taking away my position? I am too weak to dig and too ashamed to beg.
LUK_16_3.normalized: the manager said to himself what shall i do now that my master is taking away my position i am too weak to dig and too ashamed to beg

LUK_16_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_4.text: I know what I will do so that after my removal from management, people will welcome me into their homes.
LUK_16_4.normalized: i know what i will do so that after my removal from management people will welcome me into their homes

LUK_16_5.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_5.text: And he called in each one of his master's debtors. 'How much do you owe my master?' he asked the first.
LUK_16_5.normalized: and he called in each one of his master s debtors how much do you owe my master he asked the first

LUK_16_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_6.text: A hundred measures of olive oil,' he answered.'Take your bill,' said the manager, 'sit down quickly, and write fifty.
LUK_16_6.normalized: a hundred measures of olive oil he answered take your bill said the manager sit down quickly and write fifty

LUK_16_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_7.text: Then he asked another, 'And how much do you owe?''A hundred measures of wheat,' he replied.'Take your bill and write eighty,' he told him.
LUK_16_7.normalized: then he asked another and how much do you owe a hundred measures of wheat he replied take your bill and write eighty he told him

LUK_16_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_8.text: The master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly. For the sons of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own kind than are the sons of light.
LUK_16_8.normalized: the master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly for the sons of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own kind than are the sons of light

LUK_16_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_9.text: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when it is gone, they will welcome you into eternal dwellings.
LUK_16_9.normalized: i tell you use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when it is gone they will welcome you into eternal dwellings

LUK_16_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_10.text: Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much.
LUK_16_10.normalized: whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much

LUK_16_11.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_11.text: So if you have not been faithful with worldly wealth, who will entrust you with true riches?
LUK_16_11.normalized: so if you have not been faithful with worldly wealth who will entrust you with true riches

LUK_16_12.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_12.text: And if you have not been faithful with the belongings of another, who will give you belongings of your own?
LUK_16_12.normalized: and if you have not been faithful with the belongings of another who will give you belongings of your own

LUK_16_13.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_13.text: No servant can serve two masters. Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
LUK_16_13.normalized: no servant can serve two masters either he will hate the one and love the other or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other you cannot serve both god and money

LUK_16_15.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_15.text: You are the ones who justify yourselves before men, but God knows your hearts. For what is prized among men is detestable before God.
LUK_16_15.normalized: you are the ones who justify yourselves before men but god knows your hearts for what is prized among men is detestable before god

LUK_16_16.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_16.text: The Law and the Prophets were proclaimed until John. Since that time, the gospel of the kingdom of God is being preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.
LUK_16_16.normalized: the law and the prophets were proclaimed until john since that time the gospel of the kingdom of god is being preached and everyone is forcing his way into it

LUK_16_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_17.text: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for a single stroke of a pen to drop out of the Law.
LUK_16_17.normalized: but it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for a single stroke of a pen to drop out of the law

LUK_16_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_18.text: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
LUK_16_18.normalized: anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery

LUK_16_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_19.text: Now there was a rich man dressed in purple and fine linen, who lived each day in joyous splendor.
LUK_16_19.normalized: now there was a rich man dressed in purple and fine linen who lived each day in joyous splendor

LUK_16_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_20.text: And a beggar named Lazarus lay at his gate, covered with sores
LUK_16_20.normalized: and a beggar named lazarus lay at his gate covered with sores

LUK_16_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_21.text: and longing to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man's table. Even the dogs came and licked his sores.
LUK_16_21.normalized: and longing to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man s table even the dogs came and licked his sores

LUK_16_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_22.text: One day the beggar died and was carried by the angels to Abraham's side. And the rich man also died and was buried.
LUK_16_22.normalized: one day the beggar died and was carried by the angels to abraham s side and the rich man also died and was buried

LUK_16_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_23.text: In Hades, where he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham from afar, with Lazarus by his side.
LUK_16_23.normalized: in hades where he was in torment he looked up and saw abraham from afar with lazarus by his side

LUK_16_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_24.text: So he cried out, 'Father Abraham, have mercy on me and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue. For I am in agony in this fire.
LUK_16_24.normalized: so he cried out father abraham have mercy on me and send lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue for i am in agony in this fire

LUK_16_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_25.text: But Abraham answered, 'Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, while Lazarus received bad things. But now he is comforted here, while you are in agony.
LUK_16_25.normalized: but abraham answered child remember that during your lifetime you received your good things while lazarus received bad things but now he is comforted here while you are in agony

LUK_16_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_26.text: And besides all this, a great chasm has been fixed between us and you, so that even those who wish cannot cross from here to you, nor can anyone cross from there to us.
LUK_16_26.normalized: and besides all this a great chasm has been fixed between us and you so that even those who wish cannot cross from here to you nor can anyone cross from there to us

LUK_16_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_27.text: Then I beg you, father,' he said, 'send Lazarus to my father's house,
LUK_16_27.normalized: then i beg you father he said send lazarus to my father s house

LUK_16_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_28.text: for I have five brothers. Let him warn them, so that they will not also end up in this place of torment.
LUK_16_28.normalized: for i have five brothers let him warn them so that they will not also end up in this place of torment

LUK_16_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_29.text: But Abraham replied, 'They have Moses and the Prophets; let your brothers listen to them.
LUK_16_29.normalized: but abraham replied they have moses and the prophets let your brothers listen to them

LUK_16_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_30.text: No, father Abraham,' he said, 'but if someone is sent to them from the dead, they will repent.
LUK_16_30.normalized: no father abraham he said but if someone is sent to them from the dead they will repent

LUK_16_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_16_31.text: Then Abraham said to him, 'If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.
LUK_16_31.normalized: then abraham said to him if they do not listen to moses and the prophets they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead

LUK_17_1.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_1.text: It is inevitable that stumbling blocks will come, but woe to the one through whom they come!
LUK_17_1.normalized: it is inevitable that stumbling blocks will come but woe to the one through whom they come

LUK_17_2.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_2.text: It would be better for him to have a millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
LUK_17_2.normalized: it would be better for him to have a millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to stumble

LUK_17_3.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_3.text: Watch yourselves. If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
LUK_17_3.normalized: watch yourselves if your brother sins rebuke him and if he repents forgive him

LUK_17_4.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_4.text: Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to say, 'I repent,' you must forgive him.
LUK_17_4.normalized: even if he sins against you seven times in a day and seven times returns to say i repent you must forgive him

LUK_17_6.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_6.text: If you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, 'Be uprooted and planted in the sea,' and it will obey you.
LUK_17_6.normalized: if you have faith the size of a mustard seed you can say to this mulberry tree be uprooted and planted in the sea and it will obey you

LUK_17_7.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_7.text: Which of you whose servant comes in from plowing or shepherding in the field will say to him, 'Come at once and sit down to eat'?
LUK_17_7.normalized: which of you whose servant comes in from plowing or shepherding in the field will say to him come at once and sit down to eat

LUK_17_8.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_8.text: Instead, won't he tell him, 'Prepare my meal and dress yourself to serve me while I eat and drink, and afterward you may eat and drink'?
LUK_17_8.normalized: instead won t he tell him prepare my meal and dress yourself to serve me while i eat and drink and afterward you may eat and drink

LUK_17_9.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_9.text: Does he thank the servant because he did what he was told?
LUK_17_9.normalized: does he thank the servant because he did what he was told

LUK_17_10.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_10.text: So you also, when you have done everything commanded of you, should say, 'We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.
LUK_17_10.normalized: so you also when you have done everything commanded of you should say we are unworthy servants we have only done our duty

LUK_17_14.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_14.text: Go, show yourselves to the priests.
LUK_17_14.normalized: go show yourselves to the priests

LUK_17_17.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_17.text: Were not all ten cleansed? Where then are the other nine?
LUK_17_17.normalized: were not all ten cleansed where then are the other nine

LUK_17_18.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_18.text: Was no one found except this foreigner to return and give glory to God?
LUK_17_18.normalized: was no one found except this foreigner to return and give glory to god

LUK_17_19.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_19.text: Rise and go; your faith has made you well!
LUK_17_19.normalized: rise and go your faith has made you well

LUK_17_20.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_20.text: The kingdom of God will not come with observable signs.
LUK_17_20.normalized: the kingdom of god will not come with observable signs

LUK_17_21.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_21.text: Nor will people say, 'Look, here it is,' or 'There it is.' For you see, the kingdom of God is in your midst.
LUK_17_21.normalized: nor will people say look here it is or there it is for you see the kingdom of god is in your midst

LUK_17_22.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_22.text: The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.
LUK_17_22.normalized: the time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the son of man but you will not see it

LUK_17_23.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_23.text: People will tell you, 'Look, there He is!' or 'Look, here He is!' Do not go out or chase after them.
LUK_17_23.normalized: people will tell you look there he is or look here he is do not go out or chase after them

LUK_17_24.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_24.text: For just as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so will be the Son of Man in His day.
LUK_17_24.normalized: for just as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other so will be the son of man in his day

LUK_17_25.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_25.text: But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
LUK_17_25.normalized: but first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation

LUK_17_26.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_26.text: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man:
LUK_17_26.normalized: just as it was in the days of noah so also will it be in the days of the son of man

LUK_17_27.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_27.text: People were eating and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
LUK_17_27.normalized: people were eating and drinking marrying and being given in marriage up to the day noah entered the ark then the flood came and destroyed them all

LUK_17_28.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_28.text: It was the same in the days of Lot: People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.
LUK_17_28.normalized: it was the same in the days of lot people were eating and drinking buying and selling planting and building

LUK_17_29.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_29.text: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
LUK_17_29.normalized: but on the day lot left sodom fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all

LUK_17_30.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_30.text: It will be just like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
LUK_17_30.normalized: it will be just like that on the day the son of man is revealed

LUK_17_31.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_31.text: On that day, let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve his possessions. Likewise, let no one in the field return for anything he has left behind.
LUK_17_31.normalized: on that day let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve his possessions likewise let no one in the field return for anything he has left behind

LUK_17_33.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_33.text: Whoever tries to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.
LUK_17_33.normalized: whoever tries to save his life will lose it but whoever loses his life will preserve it

LUK_17_34.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_34.text: I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed: one will be taken and the other left.
LUK_17_34.normalized: i tell you on that night two people will be in one bed one will be taken and the other left

LUK_17_35.speaker: Jesus
LUK_17_35.text: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
